name: A Nature & Neighbours Network
description: A Nature and Neighbours Network (ANNE) is a community-driven organisation dedicated to giving back and supporting our community by volunteering to aid in the development and upkeep of city-wide community gardens and offering assistance to those in need. Services range from gardening to general yard care. Future goals include pop up events to distribute flowers and plants to those in the community to spread the appreciation of nature. ANNE extends its reach to fundraise for charities involved with nature preservation and those in need. To build companionship and appreciation for nature, ANNE organises a variety of team building outdoor activities. These include team gardening experiences, public park clean ups, and nature walks with community barbecues, offering members opportunities to connect with the environment and each other. These activities not only promote physical well-being but also deepen our members' appreciation for the natural world. In summary, ANNE is an organisation committed to helping our neighbours, enhancing community green spaces, and supporting local charities, all while fostering a love for nature. ANNE is open to any individuals, regardless of whether you are a student, that are interested in gardening, the environment, and uplifting their community.
summary: A Nature & Neighbours Network aims to give back to the community by volunteering at city-wide community gardens, assisting the elderly with yard care, and involvement in outdoor park clean ups to appreciate what we have and to build a better future.

name: Accounting Students' Association
description: The Accounting Students' Association (ASA) provides support for Haskayne's Accounting Students and creates opportunities for students to connect with the corporate world. Additionally, ASA provides academic support through midterm and final review packages and tutorials.
summary: The Accounting Students' Association (ASA) provides support for Haskayne's Accounting Students and creates opportunities for students to connect with the corporate world. Additionally, ASA provides academic support through midterm and final review pack.

name: ACT YYC
description: ACT YYC is an advocacy and community service centred initiative. It is focused on assisting impoverished and vulnerable immigrants, women, and youth in achieving a better quality of life.
summary: ACT YYC is focused on helping low-income and impoverished immigrants, women, and youth in achieving their short-term goals to aid them in their long-term pursuit of a better quality of life.

name: Active Aging Alliance
description: Active Aging Alliance is a student-run non-profit organization dedicated to promoting physical, mental, and social well-being among older adults and retirees in our community. We provide inclusive, engaging, and educational activities to empower individuals to maintain active and healthy lifestyles while fostering meaningful connections. Our events are centred around low-impact exercise classes, healthy aging workshops, and active social opportunities. Our commitment to inclusivity ensures all backgrounds and abilities are welcome, and activities that are tailored to meet diverse needs.
summary: Active Aging Alliance is a student-run non-profit organization dedicated to promoting physical, mental, and social well-being among older adults and retirees in our community.

name: Actuarial & Data Science Society
description: The Actuarial & Data Science Society is a student-run organization supporting students who are pursuing a career in the field of actuarial science, data science, or math. We offer students exposure to networking opportunities, interview prep, resume reviews, course resources, club events, and more! Please check out our website below for more information on how to join! https://sites.google.com/view/ucalgary-actuarial-datascience/home
summary: We are a student organization that is established for student benefit. We engage with employers nationwide and organize events for students to enhance their Actuarial & Data Science education and career prospects.

name: ADHD Achievers Association
description: Our club, which is focused on supporting ADHD kids from 8-17 years of age fosters awareness about ADHD by hosting presentations and enhancing our online presence, making regular Instagram posts that give viewers/followers information about what ADHD is, what it looks like in individuals and how as a society, we can better understand children and teenagers with ADHD. We will also work hands-on with ADHD children and teenagers by hosting events/workshops that involve mentoring (pairing volunteers with ADHD youth to help them with academic and career planning depending on their age) the ADHD youth and engaging in activities that enhance focus and overall well being (mental/physical/spiritual health) by facilitating memory games, puzzle games, mindfulness activities, sports activities, and creative activities.
summary: Supporting ADHD kids aged 8-17 years, our club fosters awareness, mentorship, and learning strategies through workshops, social events, and engaging activities that enhance focus and overall well-being.

name: Advocacy for the Ages
description: Advocacy For the Ages (AFA) is a student-run organization at the University of Calgary that focuses on raising awareness for the Calgary and Area Child Advocacy Centre, now known as the LUNA centre. The aim of the AFA is to fuel the passion in students to advocate for a safe and informed campus, concerning mental health and intergenerational sexual abuse alongside educating students and faculty about the services provided in Calgary and beyond.
summary: The University of Calgary Child Advocacy Club (UCalgaryCAC) is a student-run organization at the University of Calgary that focuses on raising awareness for the Calgary and Area Child Advocacy Centre. The aim of the UCalgaryCAC is to fuel the passion i..

name: Aesthete Dance Undergraduate Organization
description: Aesthete Dance Undergraduate Organization aims to provide the opportunity for dance undergraduate students and all other members passionate about dance to connect with each other at the University of Calgary. Through various social events, academic support, and partnerships with local organizations, we aim to enhance our members' undergraduate experience. Follow our Instagram at @aesthete_duo for the latest updates!
summary: Aesthete Dance Undergraduate Organization (aesthete) provides the opportunity for dance undergraduate students and all other members passionate about dance to connect with each other at the University of Calgary.

name: African-Caribbean Student Association
description: The African-Caribbean Student Association aims to create a campus community aimed at the celebration and the appreciation of the rich diversity, culture, and history of the regions of Africa and the Caribbean as well as its associated peoples. Everyone is welcome to join and participate in discussions, events and the community that ACSA aims to initiate and maintain.
summary: The African-Caribbean Student Association aims to celebrate the rich diversity, culture, and history of the regions of Africa and the Caribbean.

name: AGROW UCalgary
description: The Calgary Students Tree Planting Team is devoted to fighting climate change through planting trees and other plants and promoting the planting of trees. This club is able to perform the planting of trees through its multiple events throughout the year like fundraisers and awareness events. We will conclude the year with the big tree planting event in spring.
summary: The Calgary Students Tree Planting Team is committed to promote the planting of trees and other plants to fight climate change.

name: Ahmadiyya Muslim Students Association
description: We are a theological and religious organization, which is mainly devoted to promote universal religious appreciation, awareness, and tolerance to the campus community. We foster an understanding and appraisal of the worldwide community, Ahmadiyya Movement in Islam, its beliefs, its ideology, and its philosophy.
summary: We are a theological and religious organization, which is mainly devoted to promote universal religious appreciation, awareness, and tolerance to the campus community. We foster an understanding and appraisal of the worldwide community, Ahmadiyya Movem..

name: Ahmadiyya Muslim Women's Student Association
description: The Ahmadiyya Muslim Women's Student Association (AMWSA) fulfills a unique need on campus by providing a dedicated space for individuals who identify as Ahmadi Muslim women to engage in faith-based dialogue, community-building, and leadership. While other faith or cultural clubs may exist, there is currently no club that represents the Ahmadiyya Muslim Woman's perspective or provides a platform focused on the leadership, identity, and empowerment of Ahmadi Muslim women. The club will also contribute to the religious and cultural diversity of the University of Calgary by promoting respectful dialogue and interfaith understanding.
summary: The Ahmadiyya Muslim Women's Student Association (AMWSA) fulfills a unique need on campus by providing a dedicated space for individuals who identify as Ahmadi Muslim women to engage in faith-based dialogue, community-building, and leadership.

name: AIESEC Calgary
description: With presence in 124 countries and territories and with over 60,000 members, AIESEC is the world's largest student-run organization. For over 60 years, students involved in AIESEC have gained skills and competencies through challenging leadership experiences, international internships, and interaction with an extensive global network.
summary: With presence in 124 countries and territories and with over 60,000 members, AIESEC is the world's largest student-run organization. For over 60 years, students involved in AIESEC have gained skills and competencies through challenging leadership exper...

name: Alliances in Marketing
description: AIM is designed to give marketing students (and students interested in marketing) the skills, networking opportunities, and relevant experience they need to be successful, while creating a positive community for them to thrive.
summary: AIM is designed to give marketing students (and students interested in marketing) the skills, networking opportunities, and relevant experience they need to be successful, while creating a positive community.

name: Alpha Gamma Delta - Delta Nu Chapter
description: Alpha Gamma Delta is an international women's fraternity that promotes academic excellence, philanthropic giving, ongoing leadership and personal development and above all, a spirit of loving sisterhood. The Delta Nu chapter was founded at the University of Calgary in 1983 and continues to grow every year. Contact our Instagram @agducalgry for information on membership.
summary: Alpha Gamma Delta is an international women's fraternity that promotes academic excellence, philanthropic giving, ongoing leadership and personal development and above all, a spirit of loving sisterhood. The Delta Nu chapter was founded at the Universi...

name: Alpha Omicron Pi
description: Alpha Omicron Pi is an international women's fraternity committed to building lasting friendships, promoting academic excellence, supporting philanthropic initiatives, and fostering personal and leadership development. Founded on the values of character, dignity, scholarship, and college loyalty, AOII encourages members to lead with purpose and cultivate lifelong bonds of sisterhood. The Kappa Lambda Chapter at the University of Calgary, founded in 1983, proudly upholds these values and continues to grow every year. Connect with us: Follow us on Instagram @ucaoii for updates and membership information.
summary: Alpha Omicron Pi is an international women's fraternity. Our main pillars are philanthropy and sisterhood. We hold business meetings, philanthropy events, social events and develop a feeling of love and sisterhood within our chapter.

name: American Sign Language Club
description: Hey everyone! Welcome to the University of Calgary's American Sign Language Club! Do you ever think Sign Language looks interesting? Have you ever wanted to communicate with the Deaf community? This is the club for you! Our goal is to promote the use of American Sign Language (ASL) and increase awareness of the larger Deaf/hard-of-hearing community that exists both on and off campus. Through fun casual conversation events, game nights, and informative presentations we connect ASL users of all skill levels and backgrounds to learn, practice, and meet new people! You can find us on Instagram, Facebook, and Discord where we share learning resources and club updates: IG: @ASLClubUofC FB: ASL Conversation Club U of C Discord: https://discord.gg/UQk96G2Uvg
summary: Sign up! Join the American Sign Language Club to learn/practice American Sign Language (ASL) with all levels of signers, beginners and fluent. Together, we will bridge the communication barrier between the hearing and deaf students at UofC!

name: Amnesty International U of C
description: Hello! We're Amnesty International U of C, a student-run club that exists to further Amnesty International's vision of a world in which every person enjoys all the human rights enshrined in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. Our mission is to raise awareness about and generate action to prevent and end grave abuses of human rights and to demand justice for those whose rights have been violated. If you would like to join our club, please fill out the following form - http://eepurl.com/gO1oFr - and request membership here on ClubHub.
summary: Hello! We're Amnesty International U of C, a student-run club that exists to further Amnesty International's vision of a world in which every person enjoys all the human rights enshrined in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.

name: Anime Club, The
description: We're a group of anime enthusiasts who get together every week for a night of fun! We host a variety of events including movie nights, trivia, and anime Pictionary. If you have an interest in Japanese popular culture come on down and check us out! We also have a Discord server where you can chat with members of the club: https://discord.gg/2CCebhQ
summary:
We're a group of anime enthusiasts who get together every week for a night of fun! We host a variety of events including movie screenings and games.
Come join us at our Discord server here: https://discord.gg/2CCebhQ

name: AR Movement
summary: The Anti-Racism Movement's main priority is to promote racial equity and acceptance through lived experiences, as well as redefining perspectives within and between cultures, raising awareness and importance of racism within different experiences.

name: Arab Student Network
description: The Arab Student Network provides the appropriate resources, support, referral, awareness and education inspired by the enriched heritage of the Arab world, for the enjoyment and benefit of the entire student body at the University of Calgary. ASN serves as the unifying representative voice of Arab students at the University of Calgary. The club also aims to expose the culture and traditions of the Arab world to Canadian and International societies while accommodating the intellectual and social needs of Arab students and providing opportunities for leadership and career development. All the ASN activities are bilingual (Arabic & English) and open to the entire UofC community as an endeavour to initiate and foster a cultural and intellectual conversation between Arab and non-Arab students.
summary: The Arab Student Network (ASN) is a cultural organization focused on uniting the greater Arab community and those interested in Arab cultures, traditions, and languages.

name: Artificial Intelligence Club
description: Our vision is to enable undergraduates to use AI tools in any of the work they do. We envision an informed and technologically advanced undergraduate community, and we want you to be a part of it! AIC is a community that loves to talk about cutting edge technology, and how we can all use it to create cool and innovative tools. Even if you don't know how to code, or aren't in computer science, you don't need to be! There are tons of applications for AI in computer science, engineering, biology, art, medicine, agriculture, communications, education and more... As a club, we host workshops, social events, and more. Join us on Discord to keep up to date! https://discord.gg/htY8e6PBZe
summary: We want to enable the undergraduate community to harness the power of AI in all of the work they do. Regardless of the major, we will teach students how to develop AI tools, and how to use open source effectively.

name: Association of Korean-Canadian Scientists and Engineers University of Calgary Chapter
description: We provide a network of support and resources for the professional development of students interested in science and engineering. AKCSE also provides mentorship and networking opportunities between professors and young professionals, allowing students to achieve their full potential at the University of Calgary. AKCSE also provides various social events for students to interact and grow a strong support circle for their University life.
summary: We provide a network of academic and social support to aid in the professional development of students interested in science and engineering.

name: Association of Risk Management and Insurance
description: ARMI's mission is to bridge the gap between students and industry professionals in risk management and insurance. We host monthly events to foster connections, supported by a strong alumni network. Our initiatives include classroom presentations, a mentorship program, office visits, networking events, and informational booths to raise awareness about this underrepresented field.
summary: ARMI's mission is to bridge the gap between students and industry professionals in risk management and insurance. We host monthly events to foster connections, supported by a strong alumni network. Our initiatives include classroom presentations, a mento

name: Autism Awareness Club University of Calgary
description: University of Calgary's Autism Awareness Club (AACUC) strives to educate the student body and the general public about Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) by means of fundraising and volunteer opportunities. AACUC is in partnership with Stepping Out, a recreational program for young adults on the Spectrum. Through community involvement, members will have the opportunity to gain hands-on experience by volunteering with kids and young adults affected by ASD. The Stepping Out program aims to improve participants' fitness, mindfulness, teamwork, and friendship. Members will have the opportunity to work one-on-one with participants to facilitate their circuit workouts, improve their motivation, encouragement and proper form. The Autism Awareness Club will cater events that are autism-friendly and allow the student body and families to learn more about individuals on the spectrum. Events may include; art therapy night (paint night), a silent Santa, autism photo booths, sensory friendly movie nights and an Autism Symposium. Fundraising events will also be held throughout the year. All funds raised go towards the Residential Program at Society for Treatment of Autism, a therapeutic living environment for those impacted by ASD.
summary: University of Calgary's Autism Awareness Club (AACUC) strives to educate the student body and the general public about Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) by means of fundraising and volunteer opportunities.

name: Bengali Students Society
description: The purpose of the Bengali Students Society is to promote unity and togetherness throughout the university and local community. Whether it is international students, first year students, graduates, or even non Bengali students; we hope to unite everyone alike. We aim to promote the rich heritage and culture of Bangladesh through events and fundraisers while connecting with other students. This is a place to make new friendships and create new experiences. All students are welcomed to join this club! For all our club members and incoming new members, we have a Facebook group called Bengali Students Society UofC Mentorship Group. The purpose of this group is to offer all upcoming and returning students of our club with getting adjusted to the University of Calgary. We have a dedicated team of mentors from every faculty within U of C so if you have any inquiries about your degree, student life, residence, etc, you can reach out to us via the Facebook group! The link to the Facebook group is: https://www.facebook.com/groups/3490419990970565/
summary: The purpose of the Bengali Students Society is to promote unity and togetherness throughout the university and local community. Whether it is international students, first year students, graduates, or even non Bengali students; we hope to unite everyo...

name: Best Buddies UofC Chapter
description: Best Buddies is a non profit organization dedicated to creating opportunities for individuals with and without developmental disabilities to come together to form meaningful connections. As a club we plan activities and events that everyone can participate in. As a buddies students are paired with individuals from the L'Arche community in Calgary and connect with them throughout the semester.
summary: Best Buddies is a non profit organization dedicated to creating opportunities for individuals with and without developmental disabilities to come together to form meaningful connections. As a club we plan activities and events that everyone can partici...

name: Bhakti Yoga Club
description: The Bhakti Yoga Club is the UofC chapter of a Pan-Canadian university club where we discuss how to apply principles of ancient yoga wisdom to the modern way of life. Some of the relevant topics are related to yoga lifestyle, mental resilience, plant-based living, mantra meditation, conscious leadership and many more! We hope to also involve exciting and knowledgeable guest speakers to share their insights.
summary: At BYC, we discuss topics rooted in Ancient Vedic philosophy from East India. The ancient Vedic wisdom and knowledge speaks about mental health, leading sustainable lifestyles, positive character development, and career progression to name a few.

name: Bioinformatics Club
description: BINF club provides academic support for and educational sessions regarding bioinformatics research and course selection for BINF majors, minors, and students from other faculties. Through support from upper-year students, professors, and researchers in the field of bioinformatics, the club provides support for the exploration of different fields/topics in the realm of bioinformatics. Students will have the opportunity to speak with program directors and receive guidance on different careers in bioinformatics and how to achieve them.
summary: BINF club provides academic support for and educational sessions regarding bioinformatics research and course selection for BINF majors, minors, and students from other faculties. As well, it provides support for exploration of different topics in BINF.

name: Biology Students' Association
description: The Biology Students' Association is a Students' Union sanctioned Faculty of Science departmental club that provides community engagement opportunities, academic resources, and professional development for students in Biological Sciences related degrees.
summary: The Biology Students' Association is a Students' Union sanctioned Faculty of Science departmental club that provides community engagement opportunities, academic resources, and professional development for students in Biological Sciences related degrees.

name: Biomedical Engineering Research and Innovation Team
description: BMERIT is a student team driven towards creating solutions to issues to problems faced in the field of bioengineering. By working with patients and clinicians, we identify these issues and create devices and innovations that solve these issues, ensuring that these are purposeful and effective. Following this, we travel to a variety of competitions and conferences to display our work and bring awareness to the issue and the solution.
summary: BMERIT is a student team driven towards solving real problems in the field of biomedical engineering by working with clinicians and patients. We create devices and innovations that solve these issues and ensure these are purposeful and effective.

name: Biomedical Engineering Students' Society
description: The Biomedical Engineering Students' Society (BMESS) is a student-run organization at the University of Calgary for students enrolled in the Undergraduate Biomedical Engineering Program. Our goal is to provide out of the classroom learning opportunities for our students that will provide them the necessary knowledge and skills needed for their future career endeavors. We aim to connect our students with local research and industry professionals to expose them to current biomedical engineering advances. BMESS also oversees many social events throughout the academic year that unites our student body and develops a cohesive and supportive environment.
summary: The Biomedical Engineering Students' Society is a student-run organization at the University of Calgary for students enrolled in the biomedical engineering undergraduate specialization. Our goal is to provide out of the classroom learning opportunities.

name: Birthday Wishes
description: Birthday Wishes is a student-run organization devoted to providing underprivileged children from financially insecure homes with a birthday party. We believe that birthday parties can bring great joy to a child's life and create lasting memories. As a club, we provide students who have a passion for giving back to their community, a place to be able to meet and volunteer together. We partner with organizations, like Children's Birthday Miracles, to sponsor birthday parties and offer members volunteer opportunities. We also hold events for members and host fundraisers for our causes. If you are interested in getting involved, feel free to contact us through our Email or via ClubHub! Check out our Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn Pages! (Click on the Icons below)
summary: Birthday Wishes is a student-run organization devoted to providing underprivileged children (ages 4-12) from financially insecure homes with a birthday party.

name: Board Game Club
description: Please add us on Discord to become a member and receive announcements: https://discord.gg/aU5bNHd6zV The BGC aims to provide regular, weekly events for members to meet and experience the warmth and interpersonal connections fostered by board gaming. Board games offer a unique experience distinct from video games, or even tabletop games or trading card games. Video games lack the same face-to-face interaction that board games enable, and tabletop and trading card games feature significantly higher barriers of entry. Board gaming is a hobby that players of all skill and experience can enjoy, and represent themes as diverse as terraforming Mars to feudal Japanese art collecting. By holding events weekly, the BGC aims to provide students an inclusive and welcoming place to make friends, share stories, and create memories through the vast array of board gaming experiences available.
summary: A club dedicated to weekly meetups for board games, inviting players of all skill levels!

name: Bouldering Club
description: Bouldering is about 10% climbing, 90% socializing. Whether you're a new climber or a seasoned veteran, check out UCBoulder for sweet events and opportunities to connect with other students! *Please check our Discord or @UCBoulder on instagram for event details! Those are the easiest places to reach out and stay updated! To join, sign-up at this link: https://forms.gle/VWqZqjabeDmknPaWA

name: Brazilian Student Association at the University of Calgary
description: BRASA is an international, interdisciplinary group of scholars who support and promote Brazilian culture around the globe. Currently, BRASA has more than 7000 members and is present in 90 universities around the world, in countries such as the United States, England, France, China, among others. BRASA UCalgary is an extension of the organization in Calgary. The organization's ambition, until 2025, is to be a reference organization for Brazilians who study or studied abroad. We want to be recognized as leaders, entrepreneurs, and change-makers in both the public and private sectors. At the center of its values, BRASA has proactivity, excellence, meritocracy, and commitment to Brazil.
summary: BRASA at the University of Calgary is one of the chapters of the Brazilian Student Association. Our club focus on spreading the Brazilian culture throughout campus, and promoting academic and professional opportunities to its members.

name: Breathe Easy
description: BREATHE EASY is a UCalgary club committed to promoting holistic health through nutrition, science, movement, meditation, and more. ------------------------------------------------------------------------- We would love to have you! Click here to officially request membership on ClubHub, and join our growing community :) We have done some really neat events in the past including: - Outdoor yoga in the park with a yoga instructor - Online reflective journaling - Meraki Summer Fest (a huge Summer bash with live music and FREE food!) ------------------------------------------------------------------------- Make sure to follow us on Instagram @breatheeasy for all the latest updates and exclusive access to our community and events!

BREATHE EASY is a UCalgary club committed to promoting holistic health through science, movement, nutrition, and more. We aim to provide members with information, strategies, and tips through our media and events. 

Follow us on IG @breatheeasyuofc!

name: Business Pride Club
description: Business Pride Club (BPC) is a club built on an inclusive foundation to connect LGBTQ+ students and allies with firms and other individuals who are advocates of DEI in a social and professional manner. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VhLp2IHFpMc
summary: Business Pride Club (BPC) is a club built on an inclusive foundation to connect LGBTQ+ students and allies with businessmen, women, and other individuals in a social and professional manner.

name: Calgary Association of Veterinary Students
description: The Calgary Association of Veterinary Students (CAVS) advocates for the needs of students in the Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) program at the University of Calgary in academic, social, and professional regards. CAVS is a resource for DVM students needing academic and/or professional guidance. CAVS serves as a social organization, promoting networking amongst DVM students. CAVS also serves as a resource for students interested in applying to the DVM program. CAVS acts as a liaison between DVM students, the UCVM faculty, the Students' Union, and relevant professional organizations. CAVS is committed to supporting UCVM students in their transition in to the Veterinary community through financial, educational, and networking opportunities.
summary: The Calgary Association of Veterinary Students (CAVS) advocates for the needs of students in the Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) program at the University of Calgary in academic, social, and professional regards.

name: Calgary eSports United
description: At Calgary eSports United (CeSU), we are focused on bringing regular casual and competitive events to our members, as well as fielding collegiate teams that represent CeSU and the UofC in varsity competition for those who want to take their game to the next level! We will feature a mix of online and in-person events, so stay tuned on our socials for news on those! Please refer to our Discord/Insta for the most up-to-date information! You do not have to be a University of Calgary student to sign up for the club, everyone is welcome! However, you do need to be a full-time student if you want to be a CeSU collegiate player! We currently support collegiate Valorant, Overwatch 2, League of Legends, Rocket League, CS2, and Apex Legends! For the latest updates, join and communicate with others in our community on our our discord: https://discord.gg/bRTUj2SvVS
summary: Calgary eSports United (CeSU) is a video game club dedicated to promoting a connected gaming community within the University of Calgary. We will hold regular casual and competitive events for our members, as well as field collegiate eSports teams!

name: Calgary Genetic Engineering machine
description: The Calgary Genetic Engineering machine at the University of Calgary exists to spark interest and involvement in synthetic biology among undergraduate students across all disciplines. Synthetic biology is an exciting and fast-growing field that combines biology, engineering, design, and computing to build novel systems and solve global problems—from health diagnostics to environmental sustainability. Our club is designed to be a welcoming space for anyone interested in learning more about this field, regardless of their background or level of experience. We host a wide range of events, including beginner-friendly workshops, lab technique demos, guest lectures from academic and industry experts, and hands-on challenges that mimic real-world synthetic biology design processes. While the Calgary Genetic Engineering machine is not the official competition team, we work closely with the UCalgary iGEM team to share knowledge and inspire future applicants. One of our main goals is to help students build the confidence, knowledge, and collaborative spirit needed to contribute to synthetic biology innovation, whether in research, entrepreneurship, or competition. We also aim to highlight ethical, social, and global perspectives by fostering discussions on the broader impacts of synthetic biology. The club encourages teamwork, leadership, and interdisciplinary exploration, helping students see how their unique skills can contribute to scientific problem-solving. By joining the Calgary Genetic Engineering machine, students gain a supportive community, opportunities for hands-on learning, and the chance to become part of a larger movement that's shaping the future of science and technology.
summary: The Calgary Genetic Engineering Machine (cGEM) introduces students to synthetic biology through workshops, events, and hands-on activities, fostering interdisciplinary learning and preparing students for research, innovation, and future iGEM team

name: Calgary Indigenous STEAM Students' Association
description: Join us at CISSA and be part of a dynamic community that celebrates and empowers Indigenous students pursuing careers in STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics) fields. Together, we can make a lasting impact on Indigenous representation and leadership within these critical fields. Get involved, connect, and grow with CISSA!
summary: A community of Indigenous STEAM students supporting Indigenous engagement, leadership, and advancement in STEAM through mentorship, professional development, social and cultural events, volunteering, and conference attendance.

name: Calgary Ismaili Students' Association
description: The Calgary Ismaili Student's Association (CISA) is an organization dedicated to developing the physical, academic, social, and cultural well-being of Post-Secondary institution members. CISA executives are elected leaders, representatives of their constituency and representatives of the Association to the wider campus community. With access to a large Post-Secondary student group, the CISAs are in a unique position to educate, influence and provide resources to their fellow students who will one day be leaders in the wider community.
summary: The Calgary Ismaili Student's Association (CISA) is an organization dedicated to developing the physical, academic, social, and cultural well-being of Post-Secondary institution members. CISA executives are elected leaders, representatives of their con...

name: Campus Against Sex Trafficking and Labour Exploitation
description: CASTLE's goal is to educate students about the reality of human trafficking, and how we can respond to it. We organize informative events on campus, connect members with volunteering opportunities, and equip members with a proactive knowledge of the issue. This involves partnering with local anti-human trafficking organizations for speakers and volunteering opp ortunities. Our partnerships connect the campus to the community, and we work together with our partners to improve the human trafficking situation in Calgary.
summary: CASTLE brings students together against human trafficking. We raise awareness, hold informative events, and connect our members to volunteering opportunities. Our partnerships with external organizations engage students with the community.

name: Canadian Blood Services Lifesavers
description: The Canadian Blood Services Lifesavers is a club dedicated to recruiting and sustaining blood donors who are needed to meet the demands of the health care system. We host info booths in MacEwan Student Centre to answer questions about donating blood and help people book appointments to donate on our tablets. CBS Lifesavers also has an annual spring bake sale and other social events throughout the year or in conjunction with our general meetings. These meetings are held on the first Monday of every month in the evenings and are mandatory to attend. Members also undergo training to meet recruitment requirements under CBS. CBS Lifesavers has been a fully virtual club since March 2020!
summary: The Canadian Blood Services (CBS) Lifesavers is a club dedicated to recruiting and sustaining blood donors who are needed to meet the demands of the health care system.

name: Canadian Global Care on Campus
description: Canadian Global Care on Campus is a student-run organization that promotes equity in health by providing a platform for students and professionals to engage with healthcare issues and promote global wellness.
summary: Canadian Global Care on Campus is a student-run organization that promotes equity in health by providing a platform for students and professionals to engage with healthcare issues and promote global wellness.

name: Canadian Lung Cancer Screening Initiative
summary: The Canadian Lung Cancer Screening Initiative is a 501(c)(3) non-profit aimed at raising awareness about lung cancer and lung cancer screening. Through education, advocacy, and leadership we aim to to increase awareness and early detection of Lung Cancer

name: Canadian Organization for Undergraduate Health Research Calgary Chapter
description: Our goal as a not-for-profit organization is to enable students from under-represented groups throughout Canadian universities with a chance to partake in medical research opportunities. These under-represented groups include but are not limited to: low-income, refugee, immigrant, and aboriginal students. Our motive is to both enhance the chances of these students to successfully enroll in advanced academic programs (i.e MD, MSc, or PhD), while also allowing them to give back to their communities. Understanding that research experience as an undergraduate student is critical for admission to higher-level academic programs, we feel that giving these students research opportunities can act as an efficient bridge in improving both the futures of our participants and their communities. Refugee and immigrant communities in particular tend to face many healthcare barriers, some which include transportation, language barriers, lack of knowledge, and anxiety due to religious constraints. Allowing keen students of these communities to act as leaders and caregivers for their respective populations would allow for these underrepresented communities to be treated in a comfortable manner in the future. Along with this, many underrepresented groups such as Aboriginal tribes, have a lack of trust for researchers and other outsiders. Allowing members of their own communities to open up to researchers would allow for their voices to be heard and expressed through peer-reviewed journals and potential health policy related decision making.
summary: Our goal as a not-for-profit organization is to enable students from under-represented groups throughout Canadian universities with a chance to partake in medical research opportunities. These under-represented groups include but are not limited to: lo.

name: Catholic Christian Outreach
description: Catholic Christian Outreach (CCO) is a university student movement dedicated to evangelization. We challenge young adults to live in the fullness of the Catholic faith with a strong emphasis on becoming leaders in the renewal of the world.
summary: Catholic Christian Outreach (CCO) is a university student movement dedicated to creating community and evangelization, teaching and sharing the Catholic Faith, and equipping students in becoming leaders as to live out the fullness of the Catholic faith.

name: Chacmool Archaeology Association
description: Chacmool is an undergraduate club dedicated to the Archaeology students at the University of Calgary, however, anyone with an interest is welcome to join! The club is an inclusive, respectful space that creates a welcoming and accessible environment for members, and aims to actively host events for students. Club events are geared towards academics (course workshops, sharing field school opportunities, getting involved in archaeology post-grad, career fairs, teaching valuable skills for employment), and social events, where members of the club can get together and network.
summary: Chacmool is an undergraduate club dedicated to the archaeology students at UofC, but anyone with an interest can join! The club acts as a uniting force for students and actively hosts events where members can make connections and learn about archaeology.

name: Cheese Club
description: A club where we learn about and enjoy tasting different cheeses with events like cheese & game night, themed cheese tastings, and fun cheese trivia!
summary: A club where we enjoy tasting different cheeses with events like cheese & game night, themed cheese tastings, and fun cheese trivia!

name: Chemical and Oil & Gas Engineering Students' Society
description: CESS represents the undergraduate Chemical and Oil & Gas Engineering students in the Schulich School of Engineering at the University of Calgary to the school, community and the engineering profession. CESS aims to enhance the undergraduate experience and provide professional development for students enrolled in the Chemical and Petroleum Engineering Department through various academic and social activities.
summary: CESS represents the undergraduate Chemical and Oil & Gas Engineering students in the Schulich School of Engineering at the University of Calgary to the school, community and the engineering profession. CESS aims to enhance their undergraduate experience.

name: Chemistry Students' Association
summary: CHSA supports students interested in chemistry and creates opportunities to explore real-world applications through guest talks, workshops, and socials. Additionally, CHSA offers academic support through study sessions, peer help, and course reviews.

name: Children's Cottage UCalgary
description: This club is working to support Children's Cottage Society in their pursuit of building strong families by preventing harm and neglect to all children. The goal is to create a safe, fostering, and nurturing familial environment for children to thrive in. In an effort to ensure Children's Cottage Society has the resources and the personnel they need to achieve their aforementioned goals, we will support them in any way fit. We will do so in order to create awareness of, and recognition for, the amazing work this institution does for the greater Calgary community. Some examples of volunteer activities we will be engaging in include volunteering in their various programs (Brenda's House, Crisis Nursery, and Family Resource Centre), online and in person crafts, making deliveries, housekeeping (such as toy cleaning and yard cleaning), fundraising, leading events, and much more! Additionally, this will serve to provide University of Calgary students with meaningful engagement with their community, and experience in volunteer and leadership positions.
summary: This club is working to support Children's Cottage Society in their pursuit of building strong families by preventing harm and neglect to all children. The goal is to create a safe, fostering, and nurturing familial environment for children to thrive in.

name: Chinese Students' Society
description: The Chinese Students' Society (CSS) was established in 1968. We are the first and one of the largest Chinese student clubs at the University of Calgary. CSS is a non-profit club dedicated to promoting the Chinese culture, while at the same time encouraging multiculturalism within the club. CSS is also committed to creating an environment for students to make new friends and expand their social network through organized events such as our annual meet and greet, volunteer events, dances, inter-club sports events, and our year-end banquet. MISSION STATEMENT: i) CONNECT CSS aims to celebrate and promote the Chinese culture by hosting events such as Chinese New Year festivities, Hot Pot Night, Lantern Festival, and an Asian Night Market. CSS maintains a strong relationship with the Chinese community of Calgary and other cultural clubs on campus. CSS continues to look for, and are open to new collaborations with organizations. ii) SOCIALIZE CSS is committed to giving its members a great University experience. We have established an environment for students to meet, make friends, and develop a social network through events such as our annual Meet and Greet, Inter-Club Sports Tournaments, Dances, Volunteer Opportunities, and our Year End Gala. iii) SUPPORT CSS is actively involved in the community, donating our time to several charities such as Inn From the Cold, the Alberta Children's Hospital, and the Red Cross. We welcome anyone who is interested in learning more about the Chinese culture. Members do not have to be Chinese or speak Chinese to join. As a member, you will get sponsor discounts at select on-campus and off-campus businesses.
summary: CLUB OVERVIEW:The Chinese Students' Society (CSS) was established in 1968. We are the first and one of the largest Chinese student clubs at the University of Calgary. CSS is a non-profit club dedicated to promoting the Chinese culture, while at the sam...

name: Christian Students at UofC
description: We are believers in Christ. Thus, we love the Lord Jesus Christ and endeavour to give Him "the first place in all things" (Col. 1:18). Our desire is to cooperate with Him for the accomplishment of God's eternal purpose (Eph. 3:11) by ministering the unsearchable riches of Christ (Eph. 3:8) for the building up of the Body of Christ (Eph. 4:12). We warmly receive all believers in Christ and seek to maintain the oneness of the Spirit by keeping our focus on Christ. He is so rich! He is so full! He is everything to us, and He is the center of our enjoyment.We hold the faith that is common to all believers. Through daily prayer, Bible reading, and Christian fellowship we are growing "in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ" (2 Pet. 3:18). To Him be the glory both now and forever. Amen.

name: City Innovation Students' Association
summary: The City Innovation Student Association (CISA) is SAPL's undergraduate student group tasked with representing the academic and extracurricular interests of BDCI students.

name: Classrooms Without Walls UCalgary
summary: The organization aims to support the international organization Classrooms Without Walls to educate students with limited learning resources. We will focus on introducing a new perspective to students with limited access to education worldwide.

name: Cleats 4 Kids Foundation
description: We are a non-profit organization dedicated to fostering opportunities for children in need. Our objective is to afford youth the chance to engage in the sports they are passionate about, facilitating the acquisition of invaluable skills and experiences, all without the constraints of financial burden. We aspire to offer children the platform to excel and flourish through sports!
summary: An organization dedicated to fostering opportunities for children in need through sports. Giving youth the chance to engage in the sports they are passionate about and facilitating the acquisition of invaluable skills and experiences.

name: Code the Change YYC
description: Code the Change YYC (CTC YYC) will provide students interested in Computer Science and programming with opportunities to volunteer on nonprofit organizations' software projects. This will be a great way to expand your technical portfolio while giving back to the community. For students who want to get involved but have very little experience programming, we will host introductory programming workshops in many different programming languages so that everyone can work on a project that they are interested in. With a large technical project portfolio, students will be better prepared for a continuously evolving, fast-paced work environment.
summary: Code the Change YYC (CTC YYC) will provide students interested in Computer Science and programming with opportunities to volunteer on non-profit organizations' software projects. This is a great way to learn programming and volunteer at the same time.

name: Commerce Undergraduate Society
description: The Commerce Undergraduate Society is the umbrella group for all the student organizations at the Haskayne School of Business. The CUS executive serves as student representatives to the faculty, student organizations and the rest of the corporate community. We function as an avenue in which, you, the student, can build important relationships with your fellow classmates, professors, and future business contacts. Our mission is to support the Haskayne undergraduate student body while providing academic, professional, and community events and services that enhance the student experience and foster future business leaders.
summary: The Commerce Undergraduate Society is the umbrella group for all the student organizations at the Haskayne School of Business.

name: Community Garden Club, UofC
description: The UofC Garden is a community driven garden that aims to engage, share, teach and enjoy fresh food! We are a group of students passionate about sustainable living, good food, great company, and being outside. https://linktr.ee/ucgard
summary: The UofC Garden is a community driven garden that aims to engage, share, teach and enjoy fresh food! We are a group of students passionate about sustainable living, good food, great company, and being outside.

name: Community Rehabilitation and Disability Studies Student Association
description: The CRDSSA is committed to excellence. As a strong team committed to a united goal, we strive to create a better CRDS student experience from every angle. We support students' course involvement with the discord and advocacy, students' research opportunities with the annual CRDSSA Disability Research Conference, students' social lives with the HSSA/CRDSSA Gala, and students' career goals with the resources provided on our Instagram and the SIMS/CRDSSA Careers in Healthcare event. We recognize that supporting our students must be interdisciplinary and that is why we are constantly partnering with community sponsors, faculty and administration, and other SU clubs. Overall, we are a club committed to supporting CRDS students in having the most accessible, enjoyable, and fulfilling university experience possible.
summary: Our mission is to work in partnership with students and faculty to continually implement positive change in the CRDS program and create a fulfilling student experience!

name: Competitive Programming Club
description: The CPC recruits and trains students to represent the University of Calgary at the ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest. It also organizes programming contest events at the University of Calgary and provides students with an outlet and opportunity to pursue knowledge of computer science and mathematics beyond that which is available in class. Lastly, it serves also as a social gathering hub for people interested in computer science in general.
summary: The CPC recruits and trains students to represent the University of Calgary at the ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest. It also organizes programming contest events at the University of Calgary and provides students with an outlet and oppo...

name: Competitive Programming Club, UCalgary
summary: The Competitive Programming Club is a group of students, faculty, and alumni that recruits and trains students to represent the University of Calgary at the ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest.

name: Crafting for Charity
description: Crafting for Charity is a nonprofit organization run by students at the University of Calgary dedicated to improving the well-being of others through handcrafted items. Our gatherings offer a cozy setting where members come together to craft for a good cause. At the end of each semester, our handcrafted creations will be donated to those in need all across Calgary, Alberta.
summary: Crafting for Charity is a nonprofit organization aiming to spread warmth and compassion to Albertans through handcrafted items.

name: Data Science and Machine Learning Club
summary: The DSMLC aims to provide learning opportunities for students who are interested in incorporating Data Science into their skill sets. We aim to provide students with opportunities through workshops, industry nights, and competitions.

name: Debate Society, UofC
description: The University of Calgary Debate Society (UCDS) provides students with the opportunity to develop public speaking and critical thinking skills through various formats of debate. At the UCDS, we strive to help students develop these skills as they can be applied to their areas of study as well as help them succeed in competitive tournaments. There are opportunities to compete both domestically and internationally at tournaments hosted by numerous universities including the UCDS. The UCDS meets every Wednesday and Thursday from 5-7pm in CHE 110 (Craigie Hall Block-E) during the fall and winter term, discussing numerous topics ranging from politics to science, economics to philosophy, and so much more! Whether you have debated in the past or have no experience at all, our experienced members will provide training in order to help you grow your skills as a debater! If you're interested in joining or have any questions, please do not hesitate to email us at ucds.debate@gmail.com or DM us on Instagram @ucalgary.debate !
summary: The University of Calgary Debate Society provides students with the opportunity to learn, practice, and compete at debate. Whether you're interested in getting better at public speaking, improving critical thinking skills, or joining a community.

name: Digital Engineering Students' Society
summary: The Digital Engineering Student Society works to represent students interested in and/or enrolled in the Digital Engineering Minor. It will host events intended to provide opportunities to digital engineers, while also representing the needs of students.

name: Dungeons & Dragons Club
description: Join the Discord Server for Updates on Events! Chat with others on how to get started, highlights of your campaign, and more!! https://discord.gg/f6KwSsJevR The University of Calgary's Dungeons & Dragons Club's main goal is to play and share roleplaying and tabletop games, focusing but not limited to Dungeons & Dragons. The best way to stay updated on all of our events, get matched with a group, and participate in discussion is to join the official U of C Dungeons and Dragons Club Discord server.

name: Eating Disorder Awareness University of Calgary
description: Eating Disorder Awareness UCalgary (EDAUC) is a student-led group committed to supporting UofC students, faculty, staff, and the wider community in addressing eating disorders and disordered eating. Our mission is to reduce the stigma surrounding eating disorders, promote informed dialogue, and advocate for early intervention and support. We provide resources and peer-led initiatives for those personally affected or supporting someone with an eating disorder. EDAUC aims to foster a more compassionate, informed campus by: Reducing challenges faced by individuals living with or recovering from eating disorders Bridging understanding of eating disorders between youth, parents, and the community Advocating for greater research and recognition of eating disorders as serious mental illnesses Promoting healthy lifestyles free from diet culture Partner with municipal, provincial, and national eating disorder organizations in Canada (such as NEDIC, CEDAA, and Silver Linings Foundation) to promote resource sharing, collaboration, and form a network for connecting students experiencing eating disorders and/or disordered eating to find resources to support them Through workshops, events, and outreach, EDAUC strives to build a supportive network and elevate awareness of eating disorders at the University of Calgary and beyond.
summary: EDAUC (Eating Disorder Awareness UCalgary) is a student-led group supporting students, staff, and faculty with tools, resources, and awareness to address eating disorders, challenge diet culture, and promote healthy, stigma-free living.

name: Eco Club
description: Eco Club is a University of Calgary undergraduate club committed to spreading awareness and fostering rational discussions about environmental issues. We also focus on spreading environmentally ethical values and promoting a sustainable culture by raising awareness of practical and cost-effective behaviours that reduce our community's environmental impact. Join our club by clicking the "Join" button on the top right of the page. You will officially become a member, be able to collect CCR hours, and also join our mailing list which allows you to receive monthly newsletters about our upcoming events! If you are currently a member of Eco Club or would like to become a part of Eco Club, click the link below to join our roster. https://forms.gle/XrstuL5BJU8Ha4cc6
summary: We're committed to spreading awareness and fostering rational discussions about environmental issues. We also focus on spreading environmentally ethical values and promoting a sustainable culture.

name: Ecosystem and Public Health Club
description: The EPH Club is comprised of students interested in zoo medicine, wildlife, exotic animals, ecology and public health. The emphasis of the club is on learning about human/ animal/ ecosystem interactions and related issues that arise (One Health), as well as developing hands on skills that help work in this field. The club organizes and encourages participation in learning activities that include 1) Guest speakers, 2) Day trips, 3) Field courses, 4) Fundraising events, 5) Volunteer activities, 6) Conferences, and more.
summary: The EPH Club is comprised of students interested in zoo medicine, wildlife, exotic animals, ecology and public health. The emphasis of the club is on learning about human/ animal/ ecosystem interactions and related issues that arise (One Health), as we...

name: Education Students' Association
description: The Education Students' Association is the representative body for the undergraduate students in the Werklund School of Education at the University of Calgary. The organization is a bridge between the Students' Union, the Werklund faculty, and the Alberta Teachers' Association. As a registered ATA local (Student Local #2), members of the ESA receive ATA benefits such as discounts, library access, and free legal or professional support from ATA officers. The ESA provides over 100 events per year - these include 80+ Professional Development workshops to hone and expand your craft, over a dozen professional workshops and school board hiring sessions, and countless other events dedicated to socializing, physical or mental wellbeing, and equity and diversity.
summary: The Education Students' Association is the representative body for the students in the Werklund School of Education at the University of Calgary. Please note, only those registered as Werklund students are eligible to be ESA members.

name: Egyptian Student Association
description: A club where students would get to know more about the Egyptian culture, history, cuisine and music by hosting events and activities open for everyone to join. Examples of these events are meet and greets where students are going to be able to socialize and get to meet people with similar interests. As well as that, we would be organizing activities such as soccer tournaments, Kahoot, Egyptian TED X talks and riddle nights where the winning team or person would get a prize.
summary: The ESA aims to create an enjoyable community for all students at the University of Calgary, connecting them to the Egyptian culture and tradition on various levels. This also aims to help students who are currently studying abroad to feel more at home.

name: Electrical and Software Engineering Students' Society
description: The University of Calgary's Electrical and Software Engineering Students' Society, is an initiative run by students, for students. ZOO aims to support their undergraduate students, both academically and socially, by providing them with a variety of events to enrich their university experience. In addition to those responsibilities, ZOO serves as a liaison between the Department (ESE) and many engineering students. We work hard to represent student opinions and ensure that student voices are heard. Over the past few years, there has been significant growth in the students enrolling into Software and Electrical Engineering programs. Therefore, it is our goal to ensure that students are provided with the necessary resources to succeed in and out of the classroom. A smooth transition from post-secondary to industry is crucial, and it is our mission to ensure students are prepared for this transition.
summary: The University of Calgary's Electrical and Software Engineering Students' Society, affectionately referred to as ZOO, is an initiative run by students, for students. ZOO aims to support its undergraduate students, both academically and socially.

name: Enactus University of Calgary
description: Enactus University of Calgary is a student-run organization at the University of Calgary that focuses on creating positive social change by utilizing business principles. Our many programs are designed to foster entrepreneurial development, economic and environmental sustainability, and financial literacy education within the city of Calgary and surrounding areas. A head for business, a heart for the world.
summary: Enactus UCalgary is part of a global network of student-run chapters that focus on using business principles to create a positive social change in the community.

name: Energy and Environment Engineering Students' Association
description: The Energy and Environment Engineering Students' Association (EEESA) is an organization representing students in the Energy and Environment Engineering (ENEE) program at the Schulich School of Engineering. EEESA is committed to providing students in the ENEE program opportunities to supplement their classroom learning with informational and social events. Through corporate sponsorship, tomorrow's leaders in the energy and environment field will be better prepared to handle the challenges and technologies that are now emerging. EEESA is also dedicated to building a comradeship between students of all years in the program to encourage networking and facilitate opportunities for younger students in the program (or interested in the program) to interact with experienced peers.
summary: The Energy and Environment Engineering Students' Association is an organization which represents students in the Energy and Environment Engineering program at the Schulich School of Engineering. The Club also hosts academic, industry, and social events.

name: Engineering Students' Society
description: The Engineering Students' Society (ESS) works hard everyday to ensure you have the information and services you need as an engineering student. We run a store (ENE 134A) that is open from 9:00am-3:00pm, Monday to Friday, and offer a variety of services such as notes packages, merchandise, photocopiers, lost and found. Even if you are just looking for some information, come see us. If we don't have what you need, let us know and we'll do our best to make sure it's available on your next visit. ESS runs a variety of social, professional, and charity events. These events are an excellent way to network with your peers, learn more about industry, or beef up that all-important resume. We are always looking for new people to volunteer, so come by our office. ESS makes sure your voices are heard by representing students to several academic boards and committees. The Engineering Endowment, academic appeal, APEGA liaison, and Schulich Activity boards are just a few ways we make the student voice count. If you want to see something more let us know. We are always looking for new ways to expand our services. Drop by our office or contact us with suggestions.
summary: The Engineering Students' Society (ESS) works hard everyday to ensure you have the information and services you need as an engineering student. We run a store (ENE 134A) that is open from 9:00am-3:00pm, Monday to Friday, and offer a variety of serv...

name: Engineers Without Borders
description: https://youtu.be/HSXiI4myS1o Dedicated to alleviating poverty around the globe through advocacy, leadership, and combating root causes. Aside from fundraising to help end poverty overseas (Run to End Poverty) and other national ventures, EWB UCalgary focuses on a slew of local issues such as promoting Fair Trade food products, encouraging student research in environmental sustainability, advocating for sustainable laws with local government, and educating youth. Membership is free, and open to students from all backgrounds of study, not exclusively engineering.
summary: Dedicated to alleviating poverty around the globe through advocacy, leadership, and focusing on root causes. EWB UCalgary also has a robust local portfolio including promoting research, petitioning government, and engaging youth on sustainability issues.

name: Environmental Science Students' Association
description: We are a student/member powered and directed group. We host academic, social, and career oriented events for current and prospective ENSC students at UCalgary.
summary: We are a student/member powered and directed group. We provide academic services and social events to current and prospective students of the U of C ENSC program.

name: Equalities in All Qualities of Life
description: E-QOL is a student-led organization dedicated to improving the quality of life for older adults through accessibility, innovation, social awareness, and community engagement. We aim to bridge intergenerational gaps by designing adaptive and engaging games for seniors, researching systemic inequalities affecting older adults, and fostering meaningful intergenerational connections. Our engineering and innovation team leads the design of accessible games, which are tested at senior care homes. Our volunteer team hosts game nights and community engagement activities to create inclusive experiences for seniors while gathering direct feedback on our engineered solutions. Meanwhile, our research and awareness team explores the social barriers that older individuals face and publishes educational content that drives discussion and advocacy. E-Q.O.L. also organizes educational events featuring experts who address topics such as accessibility, aging, and how to navigate racial and social inequalities respectfully. Through these initiatives, we provide a platform for students and professionals to apply and develop their skills in engineering, research, and social impact, contributing to meaningful change in society. We are not waiting for a more inclusive and informed world; we are building one. One where all generations are valued and empowered.
summary: E-Q.O.L. is an interdisciplinary club that improves seniors' quality of life through accessible design, student-led research, and awareness initiatives. We engineer adaptive games and develop resources that promote equity and longevity for older adults.

name: Evren Neurological Association
description: EVREN is comprised of an interdisciplinary group of individuals who are determined to make an impact in alleviating the distorted realities surrounding various neurological circumstances through raising awareness, fundraising, research, and volunteer initiatives. The core of our efforts is to bring our team's fresh ideas and passion for raising awareness to make a difference in terms of educating individuals and providing supports for a variety of neurological disorders and neurodivergencies.
summary: EVREN is a federally registered non-profit that aims to make an impact in alleviating the distorted realities surrounding various neurological disorders and neurodivergencies through raising awareness, fundraising, research, and volunteer initiatives.

name: Faculty of Arts Students' Association
description: The Faculty of Arts Student's Association (FASA) aims to work with and alongside all students and department clubs within the Faculty of Arts in order to increase inter-department dialogue and improve student life on campus. FASA is working to increase the sense of pride students have in their faculty while developing community within the students. Working in conjunction with the Faculty of Arts, the Student's Union, and Arts student organizations, FASA utilizes every tool available to ensure that students develop a deep sense of satisfaction belonging to the largest and most dynamic faculty on campus.
summary: The Faculty of Arts Students' Association builds community, identity and works to increase campus engagement for all students in the Faculty of Arts at the University of Calgary, by hosting events, workshops and activities throughout the academic year.

name: Female Athletes in STEM Strategy Team
description: Female Athletes in STEM Strategy Teams is a non-profit organization that is run by female student-athletes focused in science, technology, engineering and mathematical fields, with the purpose of creating a supportive, knowledgeable and skilled network of students that are provided with the strategies and experiences to find success in both their academic and athletic careers. Female-student athletes often navigate unique challenges and experiences that require specific resources and tools to work through. We believe that providing these students with an environment fostered around tailored support, mentorship, and skill development, will not only improve their current academic pursuits, but also build their opportunities and development within their STEM career. FASST is committed to promoting diversity, inclusion and representation within STEM fields, to ensure that female students not only recognize their valuable perspectives and skills, but to also enrich and broaden the STEM community. This organization brings recognition and acknowledgement to the challenges and accomplishments that these individuals experience between the demands of high performance sport and post-secondary education. By supporting and empowering club members to excel in their academic, athletic and personal endeavors throughout a demanding schedule, FASST hopes to not only enhance the quality of life for students by fostering an inclusive, encouraging and innovative culture, but also positively shaping the future of STEM leadership.
summary: FASST empowers female student-athletes in STEM, fostering academic and athletic success through tailored support, mentorship, and skill development. Promoting diversity and inclusion, FASST aims to shape the future of STEM leadership at U of C.

name: Filipino Students' Association
description: The University of Calgary Filipino Students' Association (UCFSA) is a club sanctioned by the Student's Union (UofC) in 1992. Its goal is to provide its members, as well as the community, with a taste of Filipino culture. Our motto is, " Ang hindi marunong lumingon sa pinanggalingan ay hindi makararating sa paroroonan ", which means, " He who does not look back to his roots will not reach his destination ". With this in mind, the UCFSA strives to help its members realize the best aspects of being Filipino-Canadians, while engaging a variety of people and reaching out to the community. With many active members and alumni throughout the community, this club is the perfect way to meet new people and have fun in a welcoming environment while learning a little more about Filipino culture. The UCFSA is a campus hub for understanding and enriching Filipino culture/heritage bridge between students, the Filipino community and the greater society platform for active citizenship through social awareness to promote social change
summary: The University of Calgary Filipino Students' Association (UCFSA) is a club sanctioned by the Student's Union in 1992. Its goal is to provide its members, as well as the community, with a taste of Filipino culture.

name: Fish and Wildlife Club
description: The Fish and Wildlife Club is looking to offer students hands-on, in-the-field experiences to help build skills in environmental conservation and research. Our goal is to create an inclusive platform for students of all backgrounds who are interested in fish/fisheries management, wildlife, habitat reclamation, ecology, and sustainability. Whether you are looking to gain experience, explore career opportunities, or simply get involved in conservation efforts, we provide the resources and connections to help you succeed.
summary: The Fish and Wildlife Club is looking to offer students hands-on, in-the-field experiences to help build skills in environmental conservation and research.

name: Formula 1 Fan Club
summary: The goal of Formula 1 Fan Club is to create a sense of community for Formula 1 fans at UCalgary. We help bring fans together to share the excitement and thrill of Formula 1 races.

name: Fostering Change
description: Fostering Change works as an extension of 'Create Change' raising funds and awareness for youth living in poverty in Ghana. Funds raised are sent to Create Change to provide these youth with access to an education and raise future leaders within the community, with many youth provided with an education moving on to become teachers within the community. In addition to raising funds, Fostering Change also raises awareness for global development in Ghana through social media and awareness events, touching on issues such as climate change, mental health, and girls education.
summary: Our club raises money for youth living in poverty in Northern Ghana. All funds raised by our club are sent to Create Change, a non-profit organization that provides youth in Ghana with an education while promoting capacity building within the community.

name: Free Kicks Project
description: The Free Kicks Project is a student lead club dedicated to promoting early sport/recreation and physical literacy for youth. Sport allows for the development of Social and Emotional Skills, Supportive Relationships, in addition to many critical life skills. Sport can develop essential life skills, and it is our mission to support this through the following: Fundraise for organizations that demonstrate ability to provide financial assistance for participation in organized sport and recreational-related activities at both a local and international level, with goal of promoting physical literacy Promote physical literacy (motivation, confidence, physical competence, knowledge, and understanding to value and take responsibility for engagement in physical activity) importance and education for youth Provide engagement opportunities independently and in partnership with external organization for youth to try out new sports and develop physical literacy skills at no financial cost Young people have so much power. They can create positive change in their communities and carve a path to the future they want - they just need the right tools and opportunities; This is why we're here. With pieces including mentorship, development programming, access to sporting gear, and proper funds, we want to help young people take control of their life and realize their potential through physical literacy and sport. A better future starts with the right opportunities for youth.
summary: The Free Kicks Project is dedicated to promoting early sport and physical literacy for youth in Canada. We do this by providing youth opportunities to try new sports at no financial cost, in addition to partnering/fundraising with external organizations.

name: Friends of Doctors without Borders
description: UCalgary Friends of Doctors without Borders is a club registered with the National Doctors without Borders organization. We aim to raise awareness about global health issues, help crisis victims across the globe through fundraising, and promote student action to make a local and global impact. Our club aspires to foster global-minded thinking within UCalgary students and expose them to the aspect of medicine that is not discussed as often--helping populations in distress in other countries.
summary: We support the lifesaving work of Doctors Without Borders/Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF). Our Main Goals are to raise awareness of MSF and global health crises, fundraise for MSF, and encourage students to consider work with MSF to gain experience.

name: Fuse Collective
description: Founded in 2006, Fuse Collective is a multi-displinary student organization that strives to design and shape the future of sustainability. Our belief is that a sustainable future will be the outcome of collaborative efforts at the intersection of energy, the environment, business, design, entrepreneurship and a clear understanding of social and cultural impact. We strive to create hands-on experiences and learning opportunities for students to create a real, meaningful impact on their community - both on a local and global context. Click to Join our Monthly Newsletter!
summary: Founded in 2006, Fuse Collective is a multi-displinary student organization that strives to design and shape the future of sustainability. Our belief is that a sustainable future will be the outcome of collaborative efforts at the intersection of energ...

name: Future Muslims in Healthcare Student Association
description: The Future Muslims in Healthcare Student Association (FMHSA) is a safe and welcoming space for muslim students of different backgrounds passionate about healthcare and community service. This club is particularly tailored to students who aim to pursue a career in the diverse fields of healthcare, whether that is in medicine, dentistry, nursing, pharmacy, or more.
