Here, we're not only inspiring young muslims but also building a network of future healthcare professionals through mentorship opportunities , networking events , and community service initiatives . As a member, you'll have opportunities to take part in professional skills workshops, volunteering, and faith-centred events like speaker panels and Ramadan initiatives. Join us as we grow, form meaningful bonds, and make an impact both on and off campus!
summary: FMHSA provides muslim students at UCalgary interested in pursuing a healthcare career an opportunity to explore valuable mentorship, volunteering, and community-building events. We invite students from every background to be a part of this club!

name: Game Design Club
description: Game Design Club is dedicated to the creation of video games. We combine the resources of artists, programmers and musicians to foster community, create contacts, make friends, and build portfolios all at the same time. GDC's community is most active on our Discord server , where we host our online discussions, events, and meetings. New and prospective members are welcome to join and participate. For any additional inquiries, outreach, or concerns, please email us.
summary: Game Design Club is dedicated to the creation of video games. We combine the resources of artists, programmers and musicians to foster community, create contacts and friends, and build portfolios all at the same time.

name: Geomatics Engineering Students' Society
description: The Geomatics Engineering Students' Society (GESS) strives to promote the Geomatics Engineering department, foster ongoing connections with industry, and enrich the social and academic experience of undergraduate students within the department.
summary: The Geomatics Engineering Students' Society (GESS) strives to promote the Geomatics Engineering department, foster ongoing connections with industry, and enrich the social and academic experience of undergraduate students within the department.

name: Global Development Society
description: The Global Development Society is the official department club for the Global Development Studies program at UCalgary. Our mission is to EMPOWER UCalgary students with the skills, network, and capacity to become future GLOBAL leaders! Our values are " Leadership, Achievement, Community", we strive to be leaders, serve our communities, and learn from each other. F or more information about joining the GDS and upcoming events, get in touch with us through email at gds.ucalgary@gmail.com, visit our website, and join us on our social media platforms!
summary: The Society welcomes any undergraduate student of the University of Calgary who is interested in building their capacity to become a future global leader.

name: Go Baby Go
description: The UCalgary chapter of Go Baby Go is a large team of students that repairs and adapts electric ride-on toy cars for toddlers with restricted mobility, along with designing and building an innovative power mobility device. Go Baby Go operates in partnership with Variety Alberta which is a charity that advocates for children with specialized needs to provide them with greater independence. They host giveaways at which they give 30-50 modified cars at a time to families in the program, and the Go Baby Go student team assists by adapting these cars. Go Baby Go also operates in partnership with Frog Mobility, we are collaborating to adapt their current wheelchair design by adding a power systems component.
summary: The UCalgary chapter of Go Baby Go is a large team of students which repairs and adapts electric ride-on toy cars for toddlers with restricted mobility, along with designing and building an innovative power mobility device.

name: Golden Z Club of Calgary
description: Zonta International's mission is to empower women through service and advocacy. Young people are important to achieving gender equality and ending violence against women around the world. Founded in 1919 in Buffalo, New York, Zonta arose from a world where more and more women were entering the workforce and yet were not allowed to join mainstream service clubs. Zonta International's mission is to empower women through service and advocacy. Young people are important to achieving gender equality and ending violence against women around the world. And we hope you can be part of that journey with us! Golden Z Clubs are extensions of Zonta International and work within post-secondary institutions. Our club is designed to provide opportunities for young people to develop communication and leadership skills, explore career alternatives, and increase international awareness and understanding through acts of service.
summary: Our mission is to empower women through service and advocacy in a world where women's rights are recognized as human rights.

name: GoodLinks
description: GoodLinks offers University of Calgary students intergenerational opportunities that bridge the gap between youth and elderly demographics who are typically segregated within society. The club aims to break down stereotypes associated with each group and foster connections between them. GoodLinks also seeks to improve the standard of living for underprivileged seniors through charity and fundraising efforts.
summary: GoodLinks offers UofC students intergenerational opportunities that bridge the gap between youth and elderly demographics who are typically segregated within society. The club aims to break down stereotypes and foster connections between these groups.

name: Grief Gang Club
description: The Grief Gang club is an upcoming student-run organization at the University of Calgary. GGC aims at normalizing conversation regarding grief by providing a safe space for students of all faiths to learn from each other through sharing their grief journey in a non-judgmental way. We hope to create a strong community and establish connections among students to decrease the isolation that grief brings.
summary: GGC is an organization that normalizes talking about grief. It aims at providing a safe space for UCalgary students of all faiths to come together and learn from each other by sharing their grief journey in a non-judgmental way.

name: Habitat For Humanity UCalgary Chapter
summary: Habitat for Humanity UCalgary is a student-led organization at the University of Calgary dedicated to addressing affordable housing issues in the local community. Partnering with Habitat for Humanity, our mission is to mobilize student volunteers to help

name: Half Asian Persons' Association
description: Welcome to the Half Asian Persons' Association (HAPA), a warm and inclusive community at the University of Calgary for students who identify as half-Asian or with mixed Asian heritage. HAPA was created to bring awareness to the often-overlooked experience of dual heritage, a complex, deeply personal identity that doesn't always fit neatly into one box. For many of us, being mixed means never feeling fully part of any one culture, and HAPA is here to affirm that being both is whole in itself. HAPA is a space where members can celebrate who they are, connect with others who understand the nuances of dual identity, and explore what it means to belong. Through vibrant social gatherings, cultural fusion events, and relaxed hangouts, we create room for joy, storytelling, and solidarity. Whether we're bonding over shared experiences, organizing game nights, or volunteering together, we aim to foster pride, connection, and authenticity. HAPA also hopes to spark a movement: the creation of more mixed and dual-heritage clubs on campus, so that all students with multifaceted backgrounds can find community and support. We welcome all UCalgary students who resonate with our mission to join us in celebrating the beauty of complexity and the richness of being mixed.
summary: HAPA exists to unite mixed Asian students navigating the nuances of dual heritage. Through shared stories, cultural dialogues, and shared spaces, we bridge identities not to simplify, but to deepen understanding of mixed heritage.

name: Haskayne Finance Club
description: The Haskayne Finance Club (HFC) was established in 1989 and since its inception has been committed to providing students with unmatched finance-focused opportunities in order to aid in future endeavours and careers. Each semester the club hosts a variety of events which students are encouraged to attend no matter their major or year. These events include equity research presentations, financial modeling learning sessions, networking with prestigious companies, social events, and careers in finance. Students who have joined the club as either an executive, a member of the equity research program, or as a general member have gone on to do many great things. Alumni have attained prestigious internships and full-time jobs at companies such as TC Energy, RBC, PWC, Deloitte, SAF Group, BMO, Cenovus, TD, Nutrien, CPKC, Inter Pipeline, and many more. We look forward to seeing you at our next event and please don't hesitate to reach out to the below contact information should you have any questions or want to learn more. Website: https://haskaynefinanceclu.wixsite.com/my-site Email: haskaynefinanceclub@gmail.com LinkedIn: https://ca.linkedin.com/company/haskayne-finance-club
summary: Founded in 1989 the Haskayne Finance Club (HFC) is committed to providing unmatched finance focused opportunities to propel members to success in future endeavours and careers.

name: Haskayne Technology Club
description: The Haskayne Technology Club (HTC) is a student-driven organization that focuses on the intersection of technology, business, academia, and industry. We strive to inspire, empower, and connect students passionate about technology and management roles in the technology sector while increasing awareness of the diverse career opportunities in the industry. We aim to nurture business students' understanding of the tech field, expand their professional networks, and enhance their technical and soft skills, thus providing a robust foundation for their future career journeys. We are committed to providing a nurturing environment where students in need receive the support and guidance necessary to excel in academic and professional development.
summary: The Haskayne Technology Club empowers students at the crossroads of technology and business by expanding their industry knowledge, networks, and skill sets, ultimately preparing them for successful careers in business-tech roles.

name: Health & Society Scholars Club
description: The mission of the Health & Society (HSOC) Scholars Club at the University of Calgary is to enhance community connectedness through fun activities and engaging educational and career events for students majoring/minoring or interested in Health and Society alongside promoting public health issues.
summary: The mission of the Health & Society (HSOC) Scholars Club at the University of Calgary is to enhance community connectedness and facilitate fun and engaging activities for students majoring/minoring or interested in Health and Society.

name: Health Sciences Students' Association
description: The Health Sciences Students' Association (HSSA) is a club intended to enhance the social, academic, and professional realms of the Bachelor of Health Sciences student experience. Through special events and activities, the HSSA aims to foster a sense of community between students in the BHSc program, and promote the academic and professional growth of BHSc students. The HSSA also aims to increase integration of students and participation in activities between the Health Sciences Centre on Foothills campus and the main University Campus.
summary: The Health Sciences Students' Association (HSSA) is a club intended to enhance the social, academic, and professional realms of the Bachelor of Health Sciences student experience.

name: Heart 4 Heart UofC
description: The mandate of Heart 4 Heart UofC is to raise awareness about congenital heart disease (CHD) on campus and in the surrounding Calgary area. In addition, Heart 4 Heart UofC will work with our parent non-profit and their partner, Gift of Life to find life-saving surgeries for children with CHD in numerous developing countries. As Heart 4 Heart fundraises for one child at a time, Heart 4 Heart UofC will work to save each child by hosting both educational and social events for students on campus. Through our initiatives, we will not only be giving students at UofC the ability to have a resonating effect on the other side of the world, but we will be able to empower medical professional in these developing countries to provide sustainable and reliable health care for children who otherwise would never be able to afford it. An entirely student run organization, Heart 4 Heart is comprised of dedicated and socially conscious students who are passionate about helping those in need and have chosen to fight CHD in any way they can.
summary: We are the University of Calgary chapter of Heart 4 Heart, a non-profit that educates students about the most common birth defect: congenital heart disease (CHD), and raises money to fund surgeries for children with CHD in developing countries.

name: Hemoglobal University of Calgary Chapter
description: Hemoglobal®, is a Canadian charity, co-founded by Dr. Nancy Olivieri (Professor at the University of Toronto and senior scientist at Toronto General Hospital), which works to improve the medical care of children with fatal blood diseases in emerging Asian countries. The University of Calgary chapter aims to involve interested and enthusiastic students to help support the efforts of Hemoglobal®, the charity, and improve the lives of some of the most vulnerable children in Asia through advocacy events, fundraisers, and seeking sponsorships. Please follow this link to the Hemoglobal® website to learn more about the charity ( https://hemoglobal.org/ ).
summary: Hemoglobal®, is a Canadian charity which focuses on enhancing medical care for children with fatal blood diseases in emerging Asian nations. The University of Calgary chapter engages students to aid Hemoglobal® through advocacy, fundraising and more.

name: Henna Hope Fundraisers
description: The mission of Henna Hope Fundraisers is to support charitable organizations by providing henna services. This will be accomplished by raising funds through henna art application events. The events will be promoted throughout the campus of the University of Calgary. We aim to help a variety of non-profit organizations raise funds for worthy causes. Our goal is to bring the University of Calgary community together; whether you are helping the business, artistic, or event coordinating teams, you are all working for a good cause.
summary: The mission of Henna Hope Fundraisers is to support charitable organizations by providing henna services. This will be accomplished by raising funds through henna art application events.

name: Hillel
description: Our Mission: Enriching the lives of Jewish students so that they may enrich the Jewish people and the world. Our Vision: We envision a world where every student is inspired to make an enduring commitment to Jewish life, learning and Israel.
summary: Our Mission: Enriching the lives of Jewish students so that they may enrich the Jewish people and the world. Our Vision: We envision a world where every student is inspired to make an enduring commitment to Jewish life, learning and Israel.

name: Hindu Student Council UofC Chapter
summary: The Hindu Student Council is 501(c)3 non-profit that aims to provide a supportive and cultural community for Hindu students, nurturing their sense of belonging and heritage connection through empowerment, leadership, and service.

name: History Students' Association
description: The club on campus for all things historical. If you enjoy talking about history, whether you're a major or not, this is the club for you. We host social events like movie nights, trivia nights, and more. Academic assistance is offered for all members.
summary: The club on campus for all things historical. If you enjoy talking about history, whether you're a major or not, this is the club for you. We host social events like pub nights, movie nights, and trivia nights.

name: Human Resources Students Association
summary: The Human Resources Students Association aims to provide aspiring professionals the opportunity to learn how to succeed in the Human Resources field, and connect undergraduate students with resources to educate them on current and future career success.

name: Improv Club, UofC
description: Ever seen Who's Line Is It Anyway? Drew Carey's Improv-A-Ganza? Yes? Well, that's what we do! Catered to Ucalgary students of all faculties and majors, the Improv Club is designed to teach applicable skills, build confidence onstage and connect students interested in improvising in a fun and supportive environment. Come learn the secrets, perform, and hang out with us!
summary: Ever seen Who's Line Is It Anyway? Drew Carey's Improv-A-Ganza? Yes? Well, that's what we do! Catered to UCalgary students of all faculties and majors, the Improv Club is designed to teach applicable skills, build confidence onstage and connect students.

name: Indigenous Students' Circle
description: An all-inclusive organization focused on supporting Indigenous students and their relations on campus at the University of Calgary. We provide peer, cultural and educational support to Indigenous and non-Indigenous students. Attention is always paid to operating as an ethical group with respect for Indigenous cultures and histories.
summary: An all-inclusive organization focused on supporting Indigenous students and their relations on campus at the University of Calgary. We provide peer, cultural and educational support to Indigenous and non-Indigenous students.

name: Invisible Disability Education and Advocacy Association
description: The Invisible Disability Education and Advocacy Association (IDEAA) selects an invisible disability every month to focus on fundraising, educating, raising awareness, and discussing the lived experiences of those affected by it. We encourage a friendly, cooperative environment. Club members will be given the opportunity to participate in fundraising activities such as bake sales and game nights, as well as educational experiences such as information nights and seminars. If you have any questions, please direct them to our club email address: ideaaucalgary@gmail.com
summary: IDEAA seeks to fundraise and raise awareness for invisible disabilities. Club members will have the opportunity to participate in disability information sessions and fundraising activities as well as social events.

name: Irish Students' Association
description: UC Irish provides opportunities for University of Calgary students to learn the Irish language, engage with Irish culture, and learn about Irish and Irish diasporic history through language learning events, movie nights, cultural craft nights, and other similar social events on campus.
summary:
UC Irish provides opportunities for University of Calgary students to learn the Irish
language, engage with Irish culture, and learn about Irish and Irish diasporic history through social events on campus.

name: Islamic Relief Canada at UCalgary
description: Islamic Relief UCalgary is a student-run, volunteer-driven organization that aims to raise awareness in Calgary and advocate for the well-being of vulnerable communities and orphans worldwide by supporting their education, health care, food, shelter, and other necessities. We are dedicated to representing and carrying out Islamic Relief Canada's work of responding to people in need, regardless of religion, ethnicity, gender, and socioeconomic status. Islamic Relief Canada (IRC) is an internationally renowned humanitarian non-profit organization that has been providing emergency aid and long-term development projects in over 40 countries for more than 30 years. Our purpose as a student presence on campus is to inspire students to come together and make a difference in the lives of others both in our community and globally, through awareness campaigns, fundraisers, and events.
summary: Islamic Relief UCalgary is a student-run, volunteer-driven organization that aims to raise awareness in Calgary and advocate for the well-being of orphans worldwide by supporting their education, health care, food, shelter, and other necessities.

name: Jack.org UCalgary
description: Our goal as a student-run chapter is to break the stigma around mental health and create a safe environment for individuals who suffer from self-harm and suicidal thoughts. One of our main focuses is suicide prevention and allowing youth to understand the resources and ways to reach out for help. Our organization highlights how each individual has mental health. A diagnosis is not necessary to be affected by mental health. So as a group of students, we aim to have mental health and suicide talked about as a regular topic. Another objective of ours is bullying prevention with our high school Jack chapters, which can be a massive trigger for mental health struggles. Let's work together to revolutionize mental health!
summary: Jack.org UCalgary is working to support the national organization, Jack.org in its mission to revolutionize mental health.

name: Japanese Culture Collective
description: The Japanese Culture Collective (JCC) is a student-run club at the University of Calgary. Our goal is to connect students that share an interest in Japanese culture. As a club, we read books ranging from full Japanese texts and fairytales, as well as have discussions on Japanese culture. We offer members two groups to fit their learning goals: (1): Got into studying Japanese recently? Exploring Japanese texts for the first time? Our Intro to Manga group hosts Japanese comics (Manga) to utilize both imagery and text in growing your reading skills. (2): Interested in Japanese culture more broadly? Our Conversation Nights allow members to learn more about Japanese culture as well as discover some differences between Japanese and Western culture. We tackle any form of culture, and it is only limited to one's imagination. Picking a group is easy, and all members are free to take part in more than one group if they wish! We share our thoughts with our groups on a weekly basis for the intro reading nights and conversation and culture nights. Other events include social events (game nights and team bonding nights), calligraphy sessions, tea ceremonies, and other cultural ventures. In JCC, we hope you will learn something new, meet some friends, and have a great time! Join by filling out this form https://forms.gle/NZPfETvUUWWQWFPr7 And you can also join us through our Discord group https://discord.gg/DXKTAQ4299 Or Instagram! https://www.instagram.com/jcc.uofc/ If you want to contact us, please email us at jccucalgary@gmail.com or send us a message through our Instagram chat!
summary: The Japanese Culture Collective (JCC) is a student-run club at the University of Calgary. Our goal is to connect students that share an interest in Japanese culture, including literature and language.

name: K-pop Learn it Together
description: K-pop Learn it Together (K-LiT) is a dance club in the University of Calgary that aims to foster a sense of community and acts as a means for students to cultivate friendships with others who share similar interests. There will be no experience requirements, as a variety of k-pop dances from multiple diverse groups will be taught by fellow students and our VPs.
summary: K-pop Learn it Together (K-LiT) is a dance club in the University of Calgary that aims to foster a sense of community and acts as a means for students to cultivate friendships with others who share similar interests. There will be no experience require...

name: Kappa Sigma Fraternity
description: Become a Man! Join the oldest and most preferred college fraternity in the world. From its traditional Renaissance founding to it reincarnation in the 19th century, Kappa Sigma helps to promote practical life skills that will propel young men to succeed in all that they do. Based on four pillars, Fellowship, Leadership, Scholarship, and Service, brothers in Kappa Sigma learn how to live a rewarding and balanced life style while experiencing more than just your average university existence.
summary: Become a Man! Join the oldest and most preferred college fraternity in the world. From its traditional Renaissance founding to it reincarnation in the 19th century, Kappa Sigma helps to promote practical life skills that will propel young men to succeed.

name: Kinesiology Students' Society
description: The KSS, short for the Kinesiology Students' Society, is an inclusive community comprising undergraduate students passionate about the field of Kinesiology. With a robust membership base and a dedicated team of 10 executive members, our club is entirely student-driven. Throughout the year, we curate a diverse array of engaging events that cater to both the academic and social needs of our members. These events include the much-anticipated Kin Camp, the career-focused Kindustry, midweek pick-me-ups at Hump Day, and the elegant Kinesiology Gala. Additionally, we provide academic support through our peer tutoring program and foster camaraderie by hosting smaller, seasonal events throughout the year, such as pumpkin painting in October and a gingerbread house contest in December. In the spirit of unity, we cheer on our university's Dinos teams together, creating unforgettable memories and bonding experiences. Our commitment to academic excellence is exemplified through networking nights, informative student success seminars, and exclusive discounts on MCAT prep courses. As a source of pride for our club, we organize and prepare the Kin Games team, which competes at the annual Kin Games every March, showcasing the talents and dedication of our members. If you're eager to join our dynamic community, memberships are available for purchase at our conveniently located office in KNB 153, situated in the heart of the Kinesiology atrium. We accept both cash and e-transfer. Join the KSS, where we blend academic ambition with a vibrant social scene to create a well-rounded and enriching undergraduate experience in Kinesiology!
summary: We are the Society of Kinesiology undergraduate students at the University of Calgary, passionately committed to enhancing your university experience. Our dynamic events blend academics and social life. We also offer a peer tutoring program for members.

name: Kiwanis International, University of Calgary
description: Kiwanis International, University of Calgary (KIUC) is a service-based club dedicated to Service, Leadership, and Fellowship. CKI is dedicated to improving lives of children one community at a time. KIUC also contributes funding to the Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) Project to help UNICEF best supply safe drinking water to the schools and children of Haiti. As KIUC is a branch of Kiwanis International, our club will have unique connections to other generations of people who love to serve.
summary: Kiwanis International, University of Calgary (KIUC) is a service-based club dedicated to Service, Leadership, and Fellowship. CKI is dedicated to improving lives of children one community it a time. KIUC also contributes funding to the Water....

name: Korean Campus Ministry
description: Welcome to the ClubHub page for Korean Campus Ministry (KCM)! We are composed of Korean-Christian post-secondary students who share a passion and commitment for Christ. Our goal is to provide our members with opportunities to strengthen their relationship with God and bring Christ into their everyday lives. As Christian students, the majority of our "lives" consists of our time at school and so we strive to be the "salt and light" on campus to spread the good news of the gospel! We hold weekly meetings to worship/praise God, study the Bible, and pray and have fellowship! Anyone who is interested regardless of your faith is welcome to join!
summary: Korean Campus Ministry (KCM) is a place where Korean-Canadian post-secondary students throughout Calgary can come together to share their passion and commitment towards Christ and encourage each other to build the Kingdom of God within their daily lives.

name: Kula Ring
description: The Kula Ring is a social undergraduate club for Social-Cultural and Biological Anthropology. As a club, we connect students and faculty through engaging events such as fundraisers, student support events, and more. We have a shared clubroom in ES 830, where we have a self-serve tea and coffee station, as well as a microwave for members to use.
summary: The Kula Ring is a social undergraduate club for Social-Cultural and Biological Anthropology. We connect students and faculty through engaging events such as fundraisers, student support events, and more. Our club room is ES 830. Come check us out!

name: Latin America Students' Association
description: The Latin American Students' Association (LASA) promotes unity among students of different backgrounds and fosters the integration of the Latin American community. Follow us on Insta: lasa_ucalgary to stay updated!
summary: The Latin American Students' Association (LASA) promotes unity among students of different backgrounds and fosters the integration of the Latin American community. Follow us on Insta: lasa_ucalgary to stay updated!

name: Learning Beyond Borders
description: The primary objective of the student group is to establish a flow of knowledge and resources between the University of Calgary and the Ugandan primary and secondary schools. Communication through various media (film, letter, scrapbook, etc.) will be relayed to and from institutions, allowing students to connect across borders. A host of fundraisers and awareness campaigns are launched to meet the academic needs of the Ugandan schools' teachers and students.
summary: The LBB student group strives to establish a flow of knowledge and resources between the University of Calgary and the Ugandan primary and secondary schools, the basis of our cause, by spreading awareness about inequalities and organizing fundraisers.

name: Legal Bridge
description: Legal Bridge is a student-led organization that focuses on ensuring students pursuing all forms of degrees have access to resources that will prepare them for the application process to law school and activities that will expose them to the field of law. This club will deliver several interactive and informative events throughout the year in an effort to ensure that participants feel better informed about the different requirements and expectations of them and can decide if the legal field is truly the best fit for them. The community created by this club will allow students to discover other like minded individuals with whom they can connect with. Overall, this club will expose students to the legal field in a new and innovative way to encourage students thinking of pursuing law to take the leap.
summary: Legal Bridge aims to connect students in all degrees who wish to pursue a career in law with a new community.  It provides engaging actives such as networking nights, law school admissions panelists, legal case competitions, LSAT prep sessions and more.

name: Malayalee Students' Society
description: To allow University of Calgary students to celebrate and participate in traditional festivities/holidays of Kerala, especially in instances where they do not have other means to. To provide opportunities for University of Calgary students to learn more about the Malayalee culture. To allow for a space for Malayalee University of Calgary students to get together.
summary: To allow University of Calgary students to celebrate and participate in traditional festivities/holidays of Kerala, especially in instances where they do not have other means to.

name: Malaysian Singaporean Students' Association
description: The Malaysian Singaporean Students' Association, also known as MSSA, strives to bring together students with Malaysian and Singaporean roots. Hailing from two unique Southeast Asian countries with a rich, multicultural heritage, Malaysia and Singapore are truly like no other. Our goal is to share our culture for those eager to explore their heritage, as well as international students seeking a sense of belonging far from home. Anyone curious about these two small but culturally rich nations tucked away in Southeast Asia is more than welcome to join our club and community. We're particularly dedicated in assisting international students from Malaysia and Singapore in finding a supportive network, given the challenges of being far away from home.
summary: Hello! We are the Malaysian Singaporean Students Association. We aim to connect International students from Malaysia and Singapore or anyone who shares the same heritage. We also want to share our culture to those who are curious! So, makan when?

name: Man-Up for Mental Health
description: We are a diverse group of students representing many different faculties, countries, and backgrounds. One thing we all have in common is a passion for improving mental health. Man-Up has three main objectives, 1. Remove gender as a barrier when reaching out to support People of all genders face struggles and concerns in regards to their mental health. We also know that it can be especially more challenging for men to ask for help when they need, so we are working to create a campus without stigma or barriers so everyone can achieve their own mental wellness. We want to re-define the phrase "Man Up" such that manning up does not simply mean trying to hide and ignore their feelings; rather, to have the courage to connect with others in times of need. 2. Promote Safer Drinking We know that drinking can be a big part of socializing in university and for some, alcohol might be a way of coping with poor or declining mental health. We want to talk about drinking in a way that doesn't result in harm to yourself or those around you. We are NOT about stopping drinking, it's all about harm reduction. 3. Connecting Students University can be overwhelming and between classes, work, and worrying about your future, you might feel pretty alone. We are here to tell you that YOU ARE NOT ALONE! We want a campus where people can connect to those around them and we are trying to create those circumstances. Come check out an event and connect with others!
summary: We are a diverse group of students representing many different faculties, countries, and backgrounds. One thing we all have in common is a passion for improving mental health.

name: Martial Arts/Sparring Club
description: The Martial Arts/Sparring Club is dedicated to increasing exposure of students on campus to the most practical aspect of martial arts - sparring. Through careful direction and instruction, members will drill and develop technical skills, then apply them in a combat situation which provides an intense full-body workout. Beyond the physical benefits, people will find that moving out of their comfort zones in such a manner will help them to actively engage in creative thinking, improvisation, and build self-confidence. We welcome everyone, newcomers and martial arts veterans alike!
summary: The Martial Arts/Sparring Club is dedicated to increasing exposure of students on campus to the most practical aspect of martial arts - sparring. Here, people of all experience levels can learn, improve, compete, and get a great workout in with friends.

name: Mental Health for South Asia
description: Mental Health for South Asia is an undergraduate organization that aims to help spread awareness for mental health in South Asian communities while decreasing the stigma around it. Although many western societies are moving towards a status quo in which normalizing liberal social issues is standard, marginalized cultural groups, such as South Asian communities, continue to suffer the negative implications of existing stereotypes and prejudice associated with mental health. Due to this lack of empathy and complete disregard for mental health as a concept, many South Asian households have grown insensitive to the topic of mental health, alienating youth within their own households while potentially further deteriorating their mental stabilities and well-being. Thus, MHSA aims to start a healthy yet educational conversation within the South Asian community that allows individuals affected by the lack of concern for mental health to voice their opinions, be heard, and be supported.
summary: Mental Health for South Asia is an undergraduate student organization that aims to raise awareness for mental health in South Asian communities, and help reduce the stigma around it.

name: MMA Club
description: Welcome to the MMA Club where MMA come together to watch, analyze, and debate the biggest moments in MMA. Whether you're a casual viewer or a hardcore UFC enthusiast, our club is all about enjoying the sport, breaking down fights, techniques, and storylines from the Octagon. What We Offer: UFC Watch Parties: Gather with fellow fans to watch UFC in a fun and safe atmosphere. Fight Breakdown Sessions: Dive deep into fight strategies, standout performances, and controversial decisions with expert-led (or fan-led) analysis. Debates & Predictions: Argue over who really won that split decision, debate pound-for-pound rankings, and make bold predictions for upcoming matchups. Workshops: Occasionally collaborate with coaches, or analysts to share insights. Community building: Build a great community through MMA. MMA History & Film Study: Revisit classic fights, legendary rivalries, and iconic UFC moments with breakdowns and discussions.
summary:
A community for MMA fans who love breaking down the sport. Here's what we do:
Viewing Parties – Watch UFC and MMA content.
 Fight Breakdowns – Analyze techniques, strategies, and controversies like an inside
Workshops-learn more about MMA

name: Model United Nations Team, UofC
description: The University of Calgary Model United Nations Team is a twenty-year old academically competitive team. We have made our mark internationally in intercollegiate competitions through debate, engaging in current events, winning major prizes, and creating life-long friends. For more information and to answer any questions, please email ucmuntcomm@gmail.com If you're interested in joining, sign up here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSev4cEpLNhXXpdfy7uy3V4bcTX_Y5SxBAfcrakaPtVlSq2AoQ/viewform?usp=sf_link
summary: The University of Calgary Model United Nations Team is a twenty-year old academically competitive team. We have made our mark internationally in intercollegiate competitions through debate, engaging in current events, winning major prizes, and creating...

name: Music Undergraduate Society
description: The Music Undergraduate Society (MUS) gives members the opportunity to connect with the musical community of Calgary. We provide networking and work opportunities, sponsored discounts at local businesses, and academic help and resources. The MUS also hosts a number of social events throughout the school year, anything from board game nights to alumni guest speakers to our annual Christmas party! Our members get free access to all our events, as the cost of our membership covers their admission. For more information about our club, please visit our Instagram!
summary: The Music Undergraduate Society (MUS) gives members the opportunity to connect with the musical community of Calgary. We provide networking and work opportunities, sponsored discounts at local businesses, and academic help and resources.

name: Musical Theatre UCalgary
description: Tech, singing, acting, dancing, and leadership opportunities await in MTU!
summary: A club dedicated to bringing musical theatre and performance opportunities to U of C students.

name: MusicBox Children's Charity
description: MusicBox Children's Charity aims to bring music education to vulnerable children in Canada, fostering creativity and a love of music; making arts education more accessible; and promoting the development of holistically healthy children. We want to offer music education programs to children in the community, where such a program would otherwise not exist or be inaccessible. This will also provide University of Calgary students the opportunity to promote education and teach children about music through the organization's endeavours.
summary: MusicBox Children's Charity brings music education to vulnerable children across Canada. Our volunteers make arts more accessible by giving virtual music lessons at local nonprofit organizations.

name: Muslim Students' Association
description: The Muslim Students' Association is the representative body of the Muslim community at the University of Calgary, we strive to cater to the needs of our Muslim brothers and sisters to create a more welcoming and accommodating space on campus through a variety of engaging events and programs throughout the year.
summary: MSA strives to promote an awareness of Islam on campus by clarifying the ideas and concepts of Islam to both Muslims & non-Muslims. We aim to provide a welcoming social environment for students through a variety of engaging events and programs.

name: Muslimah Empowered Chapter of University of Calgary
description: The vision and mission of this club is to create a welcoming and inclusive community for all Muslimahs through academic, spiritual and social events and activities . Be on the lookout for Halaqa groups, games nights, movie nights, career networking events and much more. Let us all work together as a community to make our Dunya better but ultimately prepare for Akhirah in a safe and welcoming community. Find us on Instagram and Facebook @mecucalgary
summary: To advance future muslimah empowerment by being a catalyst for their personal, professional, and spiritual growth and to administer specialized opportunity for muslimah leadership development by pursuing female empowerment in the Calgary community.

name: Muslimah in Recreation Society
summary: Muslimah in Recreation Society aims to create a safe space for women to practice sports and physical activities. We will serve to empower and inspire Muslim women to pursue positive changes and growth. Come join a unified and fun team of Muslimah!

name: Muslims of Southern Alberta Initiating Change on Campus
summary: MOSAIC on Campus is a Muslim directed organization aimed at providing diverse opportunities for community engagement, both on and off campus. We strive to share the benefits of volunteerism, while building a strong sense of community and unique identity.

name: Muslims Without Borders: UCalgary
description: MWB UCalgary is a student-led club committed to driving meaningful change through fundraising and advocacy. Rooted in Islamic principles of charity (sadaqah), justice (‘adl), and service (khidmah), we support diverse humanitarian causes both locally and globally, including poverty, education, crisis relief, and human rights. As students, we believe change starts with us. We are not waiting for change; we are becoming it. Together, we aim to build a community of purpose, solidarity, and impactful action.
summary: MWB UCalgary is a student-led club committed to driving meaningful change through fundraising and advocacy. Rooted in Islamic principles of charity (sadaqah), justice (‘adl), and service (khidmah).

name: Natural Sciences Students' Association
description: Providing a sense of community for Natural Science students. The Natural Sciences Program is for students with a broad interest in science and through a multidisciplinary approach.
summary: Providing a sense of community for Natural Science students. The Natural Sciences Program is for students with a broad interest in science and through a multidisciplinary approach.

name: Navigators, UofC
description: We are a community of students on campus who want to provide a place where people hang out with people and seek to build a genuine Christ-centered community. Our regular events include a weekly discussion, Bible studies, community service events, retreats and conferences. Our club focus flows out of Jesus' Great Commission in Matthew 28:19-20 where he commands us to "make disciples of all nations". Join us on Friday nights 5:30-8:00pm!
summary: We are a community of students on campus who want to provide a place where people hang out with people and seek to build a genuine Christ-centered community. Our regular events include a weekly discussion dinner, Bible studies, community service events...

name: NCA Campus Fellowship
description: Welcome to our NCA Campus Fellowship! We're a vibrant community dedicated to growing in faith and fellowship. Our aim is to build meaningful connections, and making a positive impact on our campus and beyond through Bible studies, Worship and Prayer, Community and so much more. Our fellowship is a place where students from all walks of life can come together to explore their faith, ask questions, and experience the love of Christ in a welcoming and supportive environment. Join us on this incredible journey of faith, as we navigate the college experience together while grounded in the love and teachings of Jesus.
summary:
NCA Campus Fellowship is an Outreach Department of New Covenant Assembly (NCA) Calgary. 
Our vision is to support and empower Post-Secondary students in their Christian faith, as they journey through young adulthood, relationships and identity.

name: Network of Individuals Living with Mental Illnesses
description: About Us: The Network of Individuals Living with Mental Illnesses (NILMI) is a club dedicated to connecting students who live with mental illnesses, creating a sense of community. To foster those connections, we provide social events and safe spaces for discussions on living with specific mental illnesses, called peer groups. The main goal of this club is to create an environment where students feel like they are not alone in their mental health journey. We raise awareness of mental illnesses and do our part in destigmatizing them through creative outreach events. We also fundraise for other mental health initiatives. We welcome allies to join us as well! For more information visit our website: https://nilmiucalgary.wixsite.com/home Check out our latest updates on our Instagram: @nilmiucalgary @nilmicanada Check us out on X/twitter: @nilmiucalgary
summary: NILMI is a club for students living with mental disorders and allies. We connect peers together to create a judgment-free zone through social events and peer groups for specific mental disorders. We also host awareness events and fundraising initiatives.

name: Neuroscience Students' Association
description: The Neuroscience Students' Association aims to furnish a comprehensive experience to undergraduates interested in the discipline of neuroscience through mentorship and exposure to the various research areas of the field. Our club acts as a student government for the undergraduate neuroscience program, advocating for the student voice in appropriate resources and programming. Our club is also actively involved in brain health awareness and the circulation of neuroscience content to a greater audience through many collaborations with Telus Spark, Campus Alberta Neuroscience and the Hotchkiss Brain Institute.
summary: The Neuroscience Students' Association aims to furnish a comprehensive experience to undergraduates interested in the discipline of neuroscience through mentorship and exposure to the various research areas of the field.

name: New Doors for Newcomers
description: New Doors for Newcomers is a student-led organization with the focus of providing new opportunities for all newcomers, including refugees, immigrants, and international students, which will aid in their future development in a foreign land. These opportunities come in the form of networking events, job training, leadership training, day camps, and a mentorship program partnered with the Centre for Newcomers Calgary in which newcomers will be paired with university students to identify academic and personal goals by working together to formulate strategies that will ensure success. The mentorship program is subdivided into two further programs including The Real Me at-Risk Youth Program which works with newcomer youth currently involved in crime-related activities, or at risk of becoming involved in those activities. Program staff helps youth participants to reach their highest future potential, regardless of their past. Also, includes The Real Me Vulnerable Youth Resettlement Program which is designed to support Newcomer youth with relevant, timely, and critical informational resources and orientation as they settle and go to school in Canada.
summary: New Doors for Newcomers is a student-led organization with a focus on aiding newcomers with their future development in a foreign country. Allowing for growth academically and personally through mentorship program's, workshops ,and fundraising events.

name: Nigerian Students' Association
description: The Nigeria Students' Association(NSA) is a social and academic platform for students and persons of Nigeria descent and origin as well as non-Nigerians of the University of Calgary. The NSA aims to promote and share the multi-ethnic and diverse cultures, customs, and traditions of Nigerians at the University of Calgary as well that of her host communities; organize social activities and events to celebrate Nigerian Independence day and other important holidays; build a lasting relationship and communication network between Nigerians and other students and staff of the University of Calgary; develop and maintain a bridge between multicultural Nigeria and Canada; and improve and encourage the right blend of academic and social life of student both on and off campus.
summary: The Nigeria Students' Association(NSA) is a socio-cultural organization that aims to showcase the rich and diverse culture, customs, and traditions of Nigeria for students of Nigerian and Non-Nigerian descent at the University of Calgary.

name: Northern Lights Initiative
description: The Northern Lights Initiative (NLI) was founded in August 2024 to raise awareness of Indigenous humanitarian crises in Canada. NLI was developed by its parent organization, The Humanitarian Initiative, to address the challenges of highlighting Indigenous issues in mainstream Western media. Our team prioritizes increasing awareness through comprehensive and accurate research, which we share on our social media platforms in an unbiased manner. We aim to provide factual information that encourages our followers and members to critically reflect on humanitarian issues. Simultaneously, we engage with Indigenous elders and prominent Indigenous leaders to identify educational topics that need more coverage and initiatives that will help their communities. Many Indigenous communities face water and housing crises that are largely ignored or deemed as issues that can be sidelined. However, these issues have serious consequences for the affected people. NLI aims to tackle the Indigenous water and housing crises by engaging in conversations with Indigenous elders in impacted communities, listening to their perspectives on how we can assist, and developing initiatives that implement community-driven solutions. While social media plays a significant role in raising awareness and implementing change, NLI emphasizes the importance of engaging with the community through fundraising initiatives, volunteer opportunities, and educational events. Our past events, such as fundraising for local food banks and hosting seminars with Indigenous community members, were organized in collaboration with community input to ensure we take the most effective action. These events, along with our mission and goals, demonstrate our recognition that Western perspectives, which are often rooted in privilege, may not fully address the diverse needs of every community.
summary: The Northern Lights Initiative is committed to raising awareness on Canadian Indigenous humanitarian crises that tend to be overlooked by mainstream media. We aim to achieve this through fundraisers, educational events, seminars, and community events.

name: Nuclear Energy Students Community
summary: Facilitating the establishment of connections among undergraduate students with a keen interest in energy science, with a specific focus on the nuclear energy sector, in order to explore potential career opportunities, scholarships, and related avenues.

name: NōD Magazine
description: NōD Magazine is a creative publication run out of the University of Calgary campus on Treaty 7 land. We publish poetry, prose, visual art, and all intersecting forms, and we encourage submissions from new writers, previously unpublished writers, and established writers alike. We love the whimsical, eccentric, and humorous. We love the melancholic, mundane, and homely. We do not accept hateful or discriminatory work. Most of all, we love well-edited creations. NōD is always recruiting volunteers. All are welcome — no experience or U of C affiliation necessary!
summary: NōD Magazine is a creative publication. We publish poetry, prose, visual art, and all intersecting forms, and we encourage submissions from new writers, previously unpublished writers, and established writers alike.

name: Operation Smile UCalgary Chapter
description: Operation Smile UCalgary is a student-led chapter dedicated to supporting Operation Smile Canada's global mission to deliver safe surgical care and treatment to children with cleft lip, cleft palate, and other facial differences in underserved communities. Our organization actively raises awareness about the significant yet often overlooked health, psychological, and/or social challenges faced by individuals living with facial differences. The following video highlights the importance of having a student-led organization affiliated with Operation Smile Canada. https://youtu.be/Yvuii5U1JpU Through fundraising initiatives, we financially contribute to Operation Smile Canada's medical missions. This includes fundraising for critical surgeries, post-operative care, and supportive resources for patients and their families. We also provide educational opportunities for undergraduate students at the University of Calgary to explore inequities in healthcare access around the world, strengthening their advocacy skills and deepening their understanding of global health issues. Our club offers meaningful volunteer experiences, including involvement in event coordination, fundraising campaigns, and awareness projects. Through these activities, we empower students to make a tangible difference while simultaneously equipping them with valuable leadership and community engagement skills, all in support of Operation Smile's humanitarian goals.
summary: Operation Smile UCalgary supports Operation Smile Canada's mission to provide safe treatment to children with cleft lip or palate in underserved areas. Through fundraising and awareness campaigns, we empower students to advocate for global health equity.

name: Operations-Supply Chain Management Association
description: OSMA connects Operations and Supply Chain Management students with academic resources, networking and career-building opportunities to develop industry acumen, foster curiosity and build bonds to eventually thrive in the Calgarian business community.
summary: OSMA aims to connect Operations and Supply Chain Management students with networking and career-building opportunities to promote their academic and professional development.

name: Origami-Aid
description: Origami-Aid (ORI-AID) is a non-profit club dedicated to promoting community involvement at the University of Calgary. The club organizes weekly events during the academic year, in which members fold origami models and engage in conversation in a sociable atmosphere, while gaining recognition for their volunteer hours. - ORI-AID runs fundraisers and general origami events throughout the year in collaboration with non-profit organizations and clubs on campus. Through selling and donating the origami creations of its members to charity, ORI-AID encourages students to contribute to the well-being of their local community. - ORI-AID is active during the Fall and Winter semesters. ---------------------------------------------------------------------- Stay Updated on ORI-AID Events Sign up for email updates using this Google form . ---------------------------------------------------------------------- Social Media Facebook | Instagram | Website | Discord ---------------------------------------------------------------------- For inquiries, please contact ucalgaryoriaid@gmail.com .
summary: Origami-Aid (ORI-AID), is an active non-profit student club dedicated to promoting community involvement at the University of Calgary. The club organizes weekly events during the academic year, in which members fold origami models and meet new people.

name: Orthodox Coptic Student Association
description: We are a Coptic initiative within the Oriental Orthodox communion [Coptic, Eritrean, Ethiopian, Syrian, Armenian, Malankara] and we are open to dialogue and collaboration with other campus ministries and student groups. In addition to serving our own members, our OCSA chapter also aims to be a shining light to the campus community and the community at large – through initiatives such as outreach and charity which reflect the commandment of our Lord himself, “that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven” – Matthew 5:16″
summary:
OCSA is a Christ-centered campus ministry. We are open to all students
seeking fellowship and spiritual growth in the light of our Lord. We strive for
the well-being and success of each of our members: spiritually, personally and academically.

name: Outdoor Adventurers, UofC
description: We like to get outside... a lot! Pretty much every weekend, there is some sort of hiking, biking, skiing, climbing or activity going on. We also love to climb inside at the university. If any of these activities sound fun, sign up, and check out our website (http://www.meetup.com/uofc-outdooradventurers/) to receive notifications our the latest events. Also, we are one of the only clubs that are active year-round (including the summer!!). UofC Outdoor Adventurers assists people in enjoying the environment in a sustainable and environmentally friendly manner. We promote personal growth and healthy lifestyles by facilitating outdoor activities in a fun, social, and safe environment. Clubhub is only used for organizers and execs, please join our Meetup , roster/email list , or Discord to be kept up-to-date on club happenings. What we offer: A variety of mountain-based sporting events, including but not limited to: camping, hiking, scrambling, paddling, skiing, snowshoeing and rock climbing. Social meetings and other opportunities to network with UCOA members and other outdoor enthusiasts Association with the Alpine Club of Canada (ACC) which includes general ACC membership and local area membership Gear Rentals! This club is open to all post-secondary students and UCalgary alumni who are keen on nature and adventures. We practice many different outdoor activities, with different levels, throughout the entire year. You don't have to know anything to join, you just have to be willing to learn. (Also, be willing to get wet, cold, hot, sun-burnt, bug-bitten, tired, and more awesome) We are always adding new events to the calendar and encourage you to check our Meetup website regularly (https://www.meetup.com/uofc-outdooradventurers/). You can contact us at uofc.oa@gmail.com, and we will be happy to answer any questions! Join UCOA, and become a more awesome person.
summary: Explore the outdoors with UCOA! Get outside with people in a sustainable and environmentally friendly manner. We promote personal growth and healthy lifestyles in the UCalgary community. All events posted on Meetup for signup, link in description.

name: Pakistani Students' Society
description: The Pakistani Students Society is committed to promoting awareness of diverse Pakistani traditions, fundraising for charities selected by its members, helping people of Pakistani origin - particularly first year and international students - adjust to the University of Calgary in a familiar cultural environment, and to serve as an ambassador for club members on campus and in the greater community. We invite people of all nationalities to join our club and/or participate in our events and activities. We hope that you are able to make life-long friends and memories!
summary: The Pakistani Students Society is committed to promoting awareness of diverse Pakistani traditions, proving to be a home away from home for local and international Pakistani students alike, while fundraising for charities selected by its members.

name: Pangaea: Connecting Humanity
description: Pangaea's mission is to to facilitate a safe and caring environment to build international friendships and cultural understanding. One of the ways we accomplish our mission is through a mentorship program between refugees/newly landed immigrants and local Calgarians. Our mentorship program will match local mentors with a mentee that is either a refugee or immigrant new to Calgary. Throughout the year, the mentor-mentee pair will communicate with each other and work through a series of goals. These goals include cultural/religious appreciation, language acquisition, attending local events and pay it forward activities. The overall goal is that the mentor will not simply act as an instructor or role model, but will also learn about the culture, language and lifestyle of the mentee.
summary: Pangaea's mission is to to facilitate a safe and caring environment to build international friendships and cultural understanding. One of the ways we accomplish our mission is through a mentorship program between refugees/newly landed immigrants and lo...

name: Paws United Rescue Society
description: The purpose for PURS is to raise awareness for animal welfare around the university community.  Not only is animal abuse unethical to the victims, it can in turn integrate into our society.  To accomplish our goal of making the world a better place for all animals alike, we will support various animal shelters around the city of Calgary - such as the Calgary Humane Society and AARCS - that align with our vision.  In order to provide our utmost support, we will host group volunteering at shelters, fundraising activities, and other group events.  In the future, we plan to host human and animal bonding experiences to facilitate the importance of stopping animal abuse across the student body.
summary: PURS is a club dedicated to stopping animal abuse and raising awareness for animal welfare.  To accomplish this, we will support various animal shelters in the city.

name: Persian Students Network
description: Persian Students Network (PSN) at the University of Calgary serves as a social hub for Persian students, fostering a sense of community and support. Beyond this, the club actively promotes cross-cultural understanding by bringing together members from diverse backgrounds who share a common interest in Persian culture. Through social gatherings, cultural events, and professional projects, the club aims to represent and celebrate Persian heritage within the multicultural environment of the University of Calgary. Additionally, the club provides a crucial channel for communication and dialogue, creating a platform for the Persian community to engage with one another at UofC.
summary: Persian Students Network (PSN) aims to empower Persian students and unite diverse non-Persian students interested in Persian culture, representing Persian heritage through events and fostering communication within the UofC Persian community.

name: Petroleum and Energy Society
description: At PES we recognize the demand for strong technical and professional leaders in the energy industry. PES has taken steps to ensure that our student members are receiving access to professional development and resources that will help them to become future industry leaders. PES also recognizes that Western Canada is home to one of most significant hydrocarbon basins in the world, and that developing these resources in an economic and environmentally sustainable manner will present challenges for future generations of graduates. As the official University of Calgary student chapter of the Society of Petroleum Engineers (SPE), we are committed to facing these challenges by providing the energy industry with the next generation of well-rounded, inspired, and energy-driven graduates.
summary: At PES we recognize the demand for strong technical and professional leaders in the energy industry. PES has taken steps to ensure that our student members are receiving access to professional development and resources that will help them to become fut...

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description: We're a fraternity but not quite the "Animal House" stereotype that comes to mind. On Mondays we wear business attire and host a weekly dinner at our house off campus, 2459 22A Street NW, as a way to start the week off right. After our meeting we usually head to the Den to meet up with other Greek organizations, especially the lovely ladies from AGD and AOII. What sets us apart from other clubs and fraternities on UofC are quite numerous but here are some of my favourites: -we have a house with 11 rooms so if you're looking for cheap rent or a safe space to crash after a wild night we got ya covered -we have TONS of scholarships to offer; if you can get over a 3.0 (75%) GPA in your first semester with us we'll give ya $500 -we're the 3rd largest fraternity in the world so if your on an east coast trip to NYC or on a simple weekend getaway to Edmonton you'll have brothers to party with and a free place to stay at -we're heavily involved with our graduate chapter which equates to jobs when you finish university, gut busting stories from 30 years ago, and mentors available whom have experienced what you're going through With most clubs, once you graduate university you're 100% done with them. If you so choose to join Phi Gamma Delta, you'll have friends for life. We even have an annual feast called the "Frank Norris Pig Dinner" where we have all our graduate and undergraduate members sit down together for food, booze, and camaraderie. If you have trouble meeting people in university, be it that you only have lectures or any other reason, come check out Phi Gamma Delta (a.k.a. "the purple house). We do a lot of scholarship and fundraiser so if you want to kill two birds with one stone by bettering your community and padding your resume; become a FIJI. If you need a bro to wingman the beautiful lady you met at last Thursden; become a FIJI. If you want to earn sweet, sweet scholarship money whilst being held accountable to earn/maintain high grades; become a FIJI. If you want a university experience above & beyond simple test taking...we'll be waiting.
summary: We're a fraternity but not quite the "Animal House" stereotype that comes to mind.  On Mondays we wear business attire and host a weekly dinner at our house off campus, 2459 22A Street NW, as a way to start the week off right.  After our meeting we usu...

name: Philosophia: University of Calgary Undergraduate Philosophy Club
description: Philosophia is an inclusive philosophical community for anyone even mildly interested in philosophy! We cater to all levels (including, and especially, beginners) and are a great place for students to not only forge friendships but also exchange ideas.
summary: Philosophia is an inclusive philosophical community for anyone even mildly interested in philosophy! We cater to all levels (including, and especially, beginners) and are a great place for students to not only forge friendships but also exchange ideas.

name: Photography Club
summary: The Photography Club aims to share knowledge, passion, and stories through the lens! We welcome photographers of all types and skill levels.

name: Physics and Astronomy Students' Association
description: Please do not send membership requests via clubhub! We are happy to sign you up in person:) PASA (Physics and Astronomy Students' Association) is an inclusive space for undergraduate students interested in physics and astrophysics and general shenanigans. As a socio-academic club, PASA is a useful study space with whiteboards/chalkboards, a printer, and a mini-library, as well as video/board games and a great atmosphere for social tomfoolerly. Bonus: we even have a fridge and a microwave for all of your food needs! Events include Board Game Nights, Integration Bee, Cubby Brawl, Research Night, LaTeX Seminars, and other creative events! PASA is a great place to make connections, make friends in your classes, life long friends, and access to upper-year students who have the best advice. We are a kinda memey, kinda serious space for students of all types who all praise our holy Big Chungus who blesses us with luck for our tests, and pets for serotonin. You'll often find arts majors, engineers, math-loving folk, and the occasional chemist. Stop by ST 058-060 to come and enjoy one of the best clubs on campus!
summary: PASA is a lively and welcoming socio-academic club for any physics/astrophysics/math/geeky enthusiasts and friends. Our rooms are equipped with everything you need to study, look stuff up, and socialize. Find us at ST 058/060.

name: Piano Melodies Society
summary: A club dedicated to appreciating all things piano. Whether that be playing or listening to classical, contemporary or songs from favourite movies and TV shows, all are welcome to join and learn more about the piano.

name: Polish Students' Club
description: The Polish Students' Club is a great place to meet new people with similar interests and values, and we encourage anyone interested to come out to an event! (P.S. You don't have to be Polish to be a member of the club.) There is no membership fee to join the club, if you would like to become a member please contact plshclub@gmail.com
summary: The University of Calgary Polish Students' Club promotes events for post-secondary students and young professionals that are interested in learning about, as well as cultivating Polish culture. We organize fun monthly social events for our members.

name: Political Review
description: The University of Calgary's Political Review (UCPR) is an inclusive, student run organization dedicated to fostering political dialogue and academic development among undergraduate and graduate students across all disciplines. Through structures debates, internal polling, events, and most importantly, regular publication of articles on political analysis, UCPR provides a civil platform for diverse perspectives. Headed by the Executive Board, UCPR emphasizes the importance of engagement, research and professional growth amongst its cohort. The Club's mission is to create a united political community in spite of our varying ideologies, which is increasingly vital in a world that is on the upward incline of divisiveness and partisanship.
summary: UofC's Political Review is a student-led club paper and promoting political inclusivity, research and publishing. It fosters unity across various perspectives through debates and other events that encourage civil discourse and political understanding.

name: Political Science Association
description: The PSA supports all students at the university– whether it be those in the Political Science program or those who are simply enthusiasts! You can look forward to us hosting events that will help you in both your professional and your academic career, and provide you with a warm and welcoming community of like-minded students!
summary: Hey there! We're the University of Calgary Political Science Association (PSA), a non-partisan organization that represents the Political Science department here at the University of Calgary!

name: Power to Change
description: Power to Change exists to help students discover Jesus and change the world. Power to Change aims to serve the campus by being a spiritual resource for students, especially for those who wish to dialogue about issues of faith. We want to provide a welcoming environment for students to share their beliefs and to learn about connecting with God. Power to Change also aims to hold events which will serve the student community on relevant topics such as leadership, social justice issues, and other important issues in our society. We desire to raise the level of dialogue on campus about life's deepest questions and how spirituality relates to different aspects of our world and our lives.
summary: Power to Change exists to help students discover Jesus and change the world. Power to Change aims to serve the campus by being a spiritual resource for students, especially for those who wish to dialogue about issues of faith.

name: Pre-Law Women's Society
description: The Pre-Law Women's Society (PLWS) is a student-run organization at the University of Calgary dedicated to empowering undergraduate women who aspire to pursue legal education and careers. Our mission is to foster a supportive, inclusive, and resourceful community that champions gender equity in the legal field. Through a range of programming—including speaker panels, law school preparation workshops, networking events, and mentorship initiatives—PLWS provides members with opportunities to explore the legal profession, gain valuable insights from women in law, and connect with like-minded peers. Whether you're just beginning to explore a career in law or actively preparing for law school, PLWS is here to support your journey every step of the way. We welcome students from all faculties and backgrounds who share an interest in legal advocacy, professional development, and building a stronger presence for women in law.
summary: Pre-Law Women's Society is a student-led club that empowers undergraduate women aspiring to enter the legal field through mentorship, networking, speaker events, and resources that foster confidence, community, and access to legal education.

name: Pre-Optometry Students' Association
description: POSA is a club dedicated to students interested in optometry. We provide information about applications, interviews, shadowing, and the Optometry Admission Test. We host events with optometry schools, optometry students, and optometrists. As a member, you will be added to our mailing list to receive emails about events and opportunities. To become a member: 1. Click the "Join" button on this page 2. Fill out this form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfzTlwTCnIxqC3ZOXXA_YOe5APW8vImt3Z3cFprycVWNiHAVA/viewform?usp=sf_link
summary: POSA is a club dedicated to students interested in optometry. We provide information about applications, interviews, shadowing, and the Optometry Admission Test. We host events with optometry schools, optometry students, and optometrists.

name: Pre-Veterinary Students' Association
description: The PVSA aims to support students interested in veterinary medicine and provide opportunities to inform, educate, inspire their career path.
summary: The PVSA aims to give students interested in careers in Veterinary Medicine, and other animal-related careers, an opportunity for exposure to experiences and opportunities that will inform, educate, inspire them and ultimately aid them in deciding thei...

name: Project90
description: Are you interested in making the world a better place? Do you also want to develop design and consulting skills? ​ At Project90 you can do both! We are a student club at the University of Calgary that finishes hands-on design projects for local non-profits. Our projects benefit communities locally and abroad, so if you want to change the world then you've come to the right place! In addition to our year-long projects we also host career panels and discussions with Calgary's leading social entrepreneurs so that you can discover how your degree can help you change the world. We do all of this because we are a club that is based on the values of love and service as taught by Jesus Christ, and we invite everyone from every background and faith to come and be apart of Project90!
summary: We are a student club at the University of Calgary that runs hands-on design projects in partnership with local non-profits. Our projects benefit communities locally and abroad, so if you want to change the world then you've come to the right place!

name: Psychology Undergraduate Students' Association
description: PSYCHS strives to help Psychology majors get the most out of their academic career by connecting them with their peers and informing them on what they need to do to succeed. This is done through various fun events and academic info sessions that get members involved and keep them informed on how to get the most from their degree. As the undergraduate representative in department meetings, PSYCHS will keep members informed about department issues and regulations that will effect undergraduate students. We also act as a bridge to the Psychology department in presenting undergraduate concerns. To complete our membership sign-up, please complete this form .
summary: PSYCHS strives to help Psychology majors get the most out of their academic career by connecting them with their peers and informing them on what they need to do to succeed. This is done through various fun events and academic info sessions that get me...

name: Punjabi cultural Association
description: The Punjabi Cultural Association (PCA) at the University of Calgary is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community where the rich tapestry of Punjabi culture is celebrated and appreciated. Our mission is to connect youth to the roots of Punjabi culture, transcending religious or national boundaries. PCA warmly welcomes all individuals interested in or curious about Punjabi culture, regardless of background or prior knowledge. Through diverse events, workshops, and cultural exchanges, we aim to dismantle stereotypes and showcase the diversity, vibrancy, and inclusivity of Punjabi culture. Join us in building bridges, cultivating understanding, and forging lasting connections within and beyond the University of Calgary community.
summary: Fostering Punjabi culture promotion and celebration for youth at University of Calgary through events, workshops, and cultural exchanges, cultivating community bonds and student involvement.

name: Queers on Campus
description: We are queer people and allies working together to promote understanding and acceptance of queer people within the greater student, faculty, and staff community at the University of Calgary. We understand queer to include all expressions of sexual and gender diversity and welcome anyone who identifies with or who is allied with the queer community. Queers on Campus operates in three main areas: education, political action, and social events. We operate under a non-judgmental and sex-positive framework in order to build a strong and engaged network of queer people and allies on campus.
summary: Queers on Campus works to provide social events, education, and advocacy for the queer community on campus.

name: Qur'an Project UofC
