description: The objectives of Quran Project UofC are deeply rooted in preserving and promoting Quranic Arabic while addressing the educational needs of Muslim students at the University of Calgary. Our primary aim is to cultivate Muslim intellectuals who not only preserve the Arabic language but also foster a healthy understanding of the Quran and Islam within the Western context. We achieve this multifaceted goal through various avenues, including Arabic language education and the dissemination of knowledge. Additionally, we prioritize the development of social skills, community engagement, and physical well-being through activities such as volunteering, sports events, and health initiatives. By nurturing humanitarian Muslim intellectuals, Quran Project UofC also serves as a proactive measure against misunderstanding of this beautiful religion, contributing positively to both our community and society at large.
summary: Quran Project UofC strives to create space for Muslim students to foster a healthy understanding of the Quran and Islam within the Western context. We achieve this multifaceted goal through Arabic language education and the dissemination of knowledge.

name: Real Estate Student Association
description: RESA is for students from all disciplines interested in careers related to the real estate industry. RESA's goal is to provide students with education, mentorship and networking opportunity to enhance their knowledge of the real estate industry and expose them to the many different career paths. The Westman Centre for Real Estate Studies at the Haskayne School of Business work in collaboration with RESA to provide guidance and financial support for RESA initiatives. Our mission is to bridge the gap between industry professionals and students in order to promote and facilitate the growth of future leaders in the real estate industry.
summary: Our mission is to promote the growth of future leaders in the real estate industry by bridging the gap between students and industry leaders. We envision a student association that opens the door and gives students a glance into the professional world.

name: Redeemed Christian Fellowship, University of Calgary
description: RCF-UofC is a Christian organization aimed at sharing the Good News of Jesus Christ- That even though we had broken God's moral law and hated His goodness, He sent His Son to die on our behalf so that those who believe in Him as the Son of God will have eternal life and not perish.- John 3:16, 1 Corinthians 15:1-4 We all have sinned (Romans 3:23) , we have all done bad things, some big, some small, even having bad thoughts is a sin! So who is safe from God's judgment? Who is perfect? Who can stand before God and say they have only done good? No one but Jesus Christ- and it is through believing in Him and in what He did for us who hate and hated Him for His goodness, that we are made inheritors of the kingdom of God! Those who believe in the only way, the only truth, and the only eternal life, Jesus Christ, are reconciled to the King of Kings! We no longer hate Him but love Him because He first loved us!- 1 John 4:19 If you have read up until this point then I say to you a big THANK YOU! :) And if you do not understand what has been said, please do not hesitate to message the primary contact and/or come to our bible study!! :) All are welcome to join our weekly meetings!!! Thank you and have a good day :)
summary: RCF is a Christian organization aimed at sharing the knowledge of God and Jesus Christ whom He sent - John 17:3

name: ReVOLT EV Club
description: We are ReVOLT EV Club, an engineering club that aims to restore, modernize, and innovate in the electric transportation space. At the forefront of ReVOLT's mission is restoration by breathing new life into old and obsolete vehicles. Our objective is to maximize sustainability through the reuse and recycling of old components. The club specializes in the transformation of gas-powered vehicles into modern electric powertrains. Our team prioritizes innovation in our development process, aiming to implement new and untested ideas with every project we undertake. ReVOLT bridges all disciplines of engineering. Anyone involved in engineering or interested in the electric vehicle world is welcome to join.
summary: We are ReVOLT EV Club, focused on the conversion and innovation of electric vehicles. Our goal is to provide an interdisciplinary, hands-on experience in EV with an emphasis on prototyping and sustainability.

name: Right to Play
description: Want to join the greatest and biggest campus charity club? Join Right to Play Ucalgary by clicking this link to help children who are struggling in 20 different nations! You can participate in club socials, sporting events, fundraisers, and other activities, as well as leadership and volunteer positions. In some of the most underdeveloped regions of the globe, Right To Play is a global humanitarian organization that uses sports and play initiatives to help children and communities improve their health, acquire life skills, and promote peace. https://linktr.ee/righttoplayuofc
summary: Right To Play Club - Fundraising and raising awareness for Right To Play, a global humanitarian organization that uses sports and play initiatives to help children and communities improve their health, acquire life skills, and promote peace.

name: Robogals UCalgary
description: Robogals UCalgary is a Chapter of a non-profit, international student-run organization dedicated to promoting engineering, science and technology to girls in elementary, junior high and high school. Our motive is to inspire, engage and empower young women into engineering and related fields. We want to build self-esteem for women in science and engineering and target issues regarding gender imbalance in the industry. Our club specifically incorporates an outreach strategy to target youths with little to no knowledge of engineering. By doing so, it creates a larger impact and gives them an opportunity to learn something new! We aim to reach out to schools to provide free, regular workshops, career-talks and competitions for young girls to explore the countless aspects of engineering and sciences. Our afterschool workshops will include a variety of design projects, such as building a popsicle-stick bridge, constructing a balloon-propelled car, programming a simple functional robot and much more! (We plan to enlighten youth to all aspects of engineering, and hence do not limit ourselves to robotic workshops.)
summary: Robogals UCalgary is a Chapter of a non-profit, international student-run organization dedicated to promoting engineering, science and technology to girls in elementary and junior high. Our motive is to inspire, engage and empower young women into STEM.

name: Rotaract
description: Rotaract unites with Rotary International in their vision to create lasting change in communities around the world. Our club engages students in hands-on humanitarian service projects related to the Rotary Six Areas of Focus. These include building international relationships, improving lives, and creating a better world to support peace efforts and end polio forever.
summary: The University of Calgary Rotaract Club engages university students in humanitarian services and to become leaders in the community.

name: Run For Little Ones
description: Run For Little ones is affiliated with the non-profit organization Run For Little Ones. The goal of RFLO is to raise money and awareness for the Edwards Family Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU) at the Alberta Children's Hospital. Give us a follow on our Instagram account to stay up to date on the executive team applications and other news about our club! @runforlittleones
summary: Run For Little ones is affiliated with the non-profit organization Run For Little Ones. The goal of RFLO was to raise money and awareness for the Edwards Family Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU) at the Alberta Children's Hospital.

name: Rundle Group of Geoscience
description: The Rundle Group, like many other clubs, was created to provide students with the chance to optimize their University experience. Rundle organizes several yearly events, such as “Rock for Rundle”, the Department of Geoscience Christmas Party, which is always visited by Santa, a ski trip, Industry Networking Dinner, GeoSkills and the APEGA Graduation Ceremonies. Rundle also funds several field trips and tours for its members, including the Burgess Shale hike in Field, B.C. and an interpretive trip through Dinosaur Provincial Park. Furthermore, Rundle acts as a contact for students to become members of professional groups such as APEGA, CSPG, CSEG and the MEG.
summary: The Rundle Group, like many other clubs, was created to provide students with the chance to optimize their University experience. Rundle organizes several yearly events, such as “Rock for the Gallagher” (a fundraiser for the Gallagher Library), the Dep...

name: Running Is Our Therapy
description: New to running? Compete on the national level? Whoever you are, RIOT welcomes you! There's no better way to clear your mind, de-stress, make friends, and get fit than a long run at the end of the week. We run 5-12k on Fridays, rain or shine, and meet at 5:00pm in the Kines Atrium across from the fitness centre! Follow @ucriotrunning on Instagram for more information! :)
summary: New to running? Compete on the national level? Looking to run awesome trails in Calgary while meeting new friends? Whoever you are, RIOT welcomes you!

name: School Outreach Ministry at the University of Calgary
description: Transforming lives. Transforming campuses. Transforming Nations. The School Outreach Ministry (SCOM) branches from the parent organization of the Jesus Is Lord Church Worldwide. The church which began its humble beginnings in a university campus in the Philippines is now in over 60 countries around the globe. As the youth arm of the Church called the, “Christian Youth for the Nation” (CYN) we seek to expand the SCOM movement so that it may also create the same impact here in Calgary and specifically here on campus. SCOM acts as a helping hand to those who have been victims of isolation, mental health crises, and an outlet to create lasting relationships with others and more importantly, with Jesus Christ. As a result, many schools across Calgary have allowed SCOMs to operate on their campuses and true transformation and change has been evident in the lives of the youth today! Now there are currently 24 in Calgary and many more throughout Canada.
summary: The School Outreach Ministry acts as a helping hand to those who have been victims of isolation, mental health crises, and an outlet to create lasting relationships with others and more importantly, with Jesus Christ.

name: Schulich Ignite
description: Schulich Ignite is a student-led mentorship program based at the University of Calgary. Our goal is to teach high school students and beginners the basics of programming and introduce them into the world of computer science and software engineering. Our workshops introduce students to Python and other beginner languages. Mentors are available to students to explain key concepts, provide general guidance, and assist their mentees with any other questions they might have! Sessions are generally run in an 8 week format consisting of one hour and a half long sessions once a week.
summary: Schulich Ignite is a student led organization that teaches high school students and beginners to code through mentorship and lectures.

name: Schulich Soundstage
description: Soundstage is a long standing club within the Schulich school. Our aim is to encourage musicianship among U of C students, specifically catering to the engineering faculty, and to engage our members in musical opportunities and activities. Soundstage organises both formal and informal venues for open mic nights, karaoke nights, soundtracked study sessions, and various other events. We also provide interested students with paid and volunteer gigs. If you are interested in learning more just send us an email, and we will let you know more about who we are as a club and about our upcoming events!
summary: Soundstage is a long standing club within the Schulich school. Our aim is to encourage musicianship among U of C students, specifically catering to the engineering faculty, and to engage our members in musical opportunities and activities. Soundstage o...

name: Schulich UAV
description: We are Schulich UAV, an engineering club at the University of Calgary that focuses on contributing to the field of unmanned aviation by participating in design projects and collegiate competitions. The club provides members with the essential resources and education to research, design, and manufacture autonomous aircraft using advanced materials and technologies. We aim to create a community of enthusiasts and to increase the awareness of the applications of autonomous aircraft in industry. The club is open to everyone and there are currently members from every faculty as there are always interesting projects to work on. Anyone with an interest in engineering, computer science, business, or just aviation, would be a great fit here at Schulich UAV.
summary: We are Schulich UAV, an engineering club at the University of Calgary that focuses on contributing to the field of unmanned aviation by participating in design projects and collegiate competitions.

name: Science FUNdamentals Foundation of Alberta: Calgary Chapter
description: Science FUNdamentals (SciFUN) was originally established as a student group at the University of Alberta (UofA) in 2003 with the mandate to “provide accessible science education for all” by creating fun and interactive science programming for grades K-6. Today, SciFUN is a CRA registered charity with an expanded programming to include kindergarten to grade 6 students. Our mission is fulfilled primarily through two of our initiatives: year-round classroom programming and Science FUNday. In our year-round classroom programming, our volunteers are paired up and assigned to an elementary school classroom for the year to present monthly interactive science demos. Our volunteers have backgrounds ranging from education to sciences and arts faculty, and we serve 100 different elementary schools across the city/rural areas. With demos ranging from strawberry DNA extraction to making paper, our volunteers supplement the existing provincial curriculum and enrich the learning experience in primary education. Since our classroom placements last for a minimum of 4 months, the volunteers are able to form friendly connections with the students and facilitate a familiar and comfortable environment for inquisitive learning. Our former volunteers have also successfully fostered professional connections with their respective teachers and have benefited from them in terms of guidance in career development. Aside from experience in an educational setting, our volunteers also gain valuable scientific communication skills which have been useful for those who go on to graduate school and present their research to academic audiences. The volunteers generally report feeling more confident with public speaking after working with our organization. Testimonials from elementary students come in the form of handmade cards at the end of the school year that really show us the positive impact of our interactive approach to science education.
summary: SciFUN UofC is a branch of the Science FUNdamentals Foundation of Alberta, established at the University of Alberta with the mandate to “provide accessible science education for all” by creating fun and interactive science programming for grades K-6

name: Shenanigans! Club
description: CLICK HERE TO BECOME A MEMBER! https://forms.gle/HFzQUQqp9XwpRKQH8 Shenanigans! is a Students' Union sanctioned club. We meet at least once a month to simply hang out, relax, de-stress, and have fun with casual games. We're all about inclusivity, fair play, and respect. If you would like to join our club, we ask that you adhere to these principles. Some of the main games we play include board games, a variety of tag games, capture the flag, outdoor sports, and hide and seek; we occasionally plan larger events too such as skating, video games, and badminton. All events are either held on campus or virtually (through Discord). We will send out emails about upcoming events; in addition, please click the icons below the email link to reach our Discord, Instagram and Facebook pages for more information and discussion! And for our Club's Discord, Facebook and Instagram, click the little icons below!
summary: Shenanigans! is a Students' Union club that holds events at least once a month to simply hang out, play games, and have fun! Some of our past events include variants of tag, capture the flag, scavenger hunts, and ice skating.

name: Sikh Students Association
description: The Sikh Students' Association is a student club that works to collectively make a positive change on campus and within the larger community while spreading awareness of the principles and foundations of what Sikhi is based upon. Follow us @ssauofc on Instagram for club updates!
summary: The Sikh Students' Association is a student club that works to collectively make a positive change on campus and within the larger community while spreading awareness of the principles and foundations of what Sikhi is based upon.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers at uCalgary
description: SASE at uCalgary is the first Canadian student chapter of the Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers. Our Vision is to foster a community that supports the personal and professional development of Asian heritage students pursuing a degree in or who holds interest in the fields of Science and Engineering. Our mission statements are: Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world. Celebrate diversity on campuses and in the workplace. Provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities. Educate about systemic barriers students of Asian heritage face in the professional world and how to overcome and/or eliminate them. SASE at uCalgary will enable students to develop their personal and professional goals by creating opportunities for them to learn from practical experiences on the University of Calgary campus.
summary: SASE at uCalgary aims to foster the professional development of Asian heritage students in Science and Engineering by creating mentorship and networking opportunities for them to learn from practical experiences.

name: Society of Energy Engineers
description: The University of Calgary Society of Energy Engineers (SEE) is a department student club run by undergraduate students with the fundamental purpose of creating a diverse, technical, and connected student workforce. We believe that by equipping our students with a solid foundation of engineering principals, knowledge, experience, and networking skills, they will gain a competitive advantage when entering any industry. We host events, mixers, international trips, and much more!
summary: Department Student Club for the Energy Engineering program at the Schulich School of Engineering.

name: Society of Undergraduate Mathematics - Calgary
description: Check out our website at sumc.ca! The Society of Undergraduate Mathematics-Calgary (SUM-C) is the longest-lasting undergraduate math club in the city. It is one of the longest-running non-profit organizations at the University of Calgary. Our mission is to serve the undergraduate mathematics community, share the love of mathematics, and help every student find success and belonging in mathematics. If you're struggling with math, looking for jobs in mathematics, or looking to excel in research, we have a home for you! We provide academic resources, career networking, undergraduate research seminars, leadership opportunities, outreach, and an exceptional community for pursuing mathematical excellence. We provide a radical environment to meet new people, study intensely, or snag a power nap after a super long class! Also, we can point you in the direction of a math tutor to help you succeed, or a textbook with a different angle of approach. Finally, we publish an almost weekly newsletter filled with awesome events and announcements that could spice up your life. Whatever your heart desires, we might have it. So join our Discord!: https://discord.gg/65qRuEwZXf Contact us at sumc@ucalgary.ca!
summary: UCalgary's Undergraduate Math Club!

name: Society of Undergraduates in Economics
description: Welcome to the Society of Undergraduates in Economics. Our mission is to serve the career, academic and social needs of students enrolled in economics as well as those with an interest in economics. We host a broad range of events throughout the year with these goals in mind. In particular, the SUE executive serves as a liaison between students and the Department of Economics, the Economics Society of Calgary and other industry associations. In representing student interests, the SUE has the following roles: - Assisting students in their studies through our online exam bank, student mentorship, and office hours - Preparing students for their careers by hosting industry nights, resume clinics and information sessions - Advocating on behalf of undergraduate students to the department for higher quality education. Please explore our website and social media sites at www.ucalgarysue.com and www.facebook.com/ucalgarysue or email us at societyofundergradsineconomics@gmail.com to learn more.
summary: Welcome to the Society of Undergraduates in Economics.Our mission is to serve the career, academic and social needs of students enrolled in economics as well as those with an interest in economics. We host a broad range of events throughout the year wi...

name: Sri Lankan Students' Association
description: The Sri Lankan Students Society at the University of Calgary celebrates the rich tapestry of Sri Lankan culture. We provide an inclusive space for students to share experiences and deepen their understanding of social customs, arts, cuisine, and customs.
summary: The Sri Lankan Students Society at the University of Calgary celebrates the rich tapestry of Sri Lankan culture. We provide an inclusive space for students to share experiences and deepen their understanding of social customs, arts, cuisine, and customs.

name: St. Francis Xavier Chaplaincy at the University of Calgary
description: The St. Francis Xavier Chaplaincy is a Catholic group on campus that fosters a community for Catholic students. We engage in lots of fun events on campus and St. Mary's Cathedral. We have faith formation events such as faith seminars and testimony nights. We also have social events such as game nights and fellowship. Come on out and meet up with some fellow Catholics on campus! Everyone is welcome, membership is free!
summary: The St. Francis Xavier Chaplaincy is a Catholic group on campus that fosters a community for Catholic students. We engage in lots of fun events on campus and St. Mary's Cathedral. Come on out and meet up with some fellow Catholics!

name: Stars for Scholarly Youth
description: Welcome to SSY! Our goal is to equalize opportunities in the pursuit of education and self-development for underserved and vulnerable youth (grades 5 - 12). Examples include youth in foster homes and other community interventions as well as youth who would not have the financial ability to afford these programs. There are many ways that YOU can get involved! We maintain a free tutoring network, serving the youth directed to us from partner NPOs and the community, where you can sign up to be a 1-on-1 tutor. We also have a English Language Literacy program to support newcomer and immigrant youth with English language skills. Our mentorship program though still connected with us can now be found under "StarGATE Mentorship" on ClubHub. We are always looking for people interested in helping others like YOU so sign up here!
summary: We seek to equalize opportunities in the pursuit of education and self-development for underserved youth (grades 5 - 12) who are involved in community interventions and/ or don't have the financial capacity by creating a tutoring and mentorship network.

name: STEM Art Hub
description: STEM Art Hub (S.A. Hub) aims to create an open-minded platform for students in STEM to cultivate artistic curiosity, while simultaneously fostering an appreciation for the arts. S.A. Hub will create opportunities for students to engage in creative processes that they might not have had the chance to otherwise. S.A. Hub primarily serves Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math (STEM) students, although all undergraduate University of Calgary students are welcome. STEM Art Hub will do this by targeting mental health for students by developing art-based stress-reducing habits, fostering an appreciation for the arts within our school community (partnerships with University of Calgary affiliations) and our community at-large by providing art service, cultivating an understanding of how participating in the arts can actually benefit STEM students, how art makes you a better thinker and more well-rounded, and bridging the gap between art and science. LinkTree for Membership Application: https://linktr.ee/stem.art.hub Our Website: https://uofcstemarthub.edublogs.org/
summary: STEM Art Hub aims to create an open-minded platform for students in STEM to cultivate artistic curiosity, as well as to bridge the gap between the arts and sciences.

name: STEM Fellowship
description: STEM Fellowship is a student-run, national organization that provides a platform for students interested in STEM fields to connect with one another and with professionals, engage in research and improve skills in scholarly writing and data sciences. We seek to enable students to learn collaboratively, express their passion and love for science, and expand the scope of human knowledge through scientific, multidisciplinary inquiry. The U of C STEM Fellowship branch holds scholarly writing and research workshops where students can participate in interactive activities to learn tips on writing scientific journal articles or getting involved in research. We also coordinate networking events to allow students to learn from the experiences of upper year undergraduates and interact with other like-minded peers. Additionally, we organize a high school research internship program which enables high school students from communities underrepresented in STEM pursuits to conduct research at the U of C for a week during spring break.
summary: STEM Fellowship is a student-run, national organization that provides a platform for students interested in STEM fields to connect with one another and with professionals, engage in research and improve skills in scholarly writing and data sciences. We...

name: Student Energy Chapter at University of Calgary
description: The purpose of Student Energy UofC is as follows: To bring awareness of clean energy to the students at the University of Calgary, in order to foster a new generation of aspiring leaders in renewable energy. To create and support initiatives that strive to improve energy literacy, as well as, increase sustainability and environmental awareness at the University of Calgary. To enhance the experience and knowledge of students in the Schulich School of Engineering, as well as, at the University of Calgary by providing opportunities: to learn about clean energy through events; to develop technical knowledge and skills in the fields of clean energy; and for professional development.
summary: The purpose of Student Energy is to bring awareness of clean energy and foster a new generation of aspiring leaders in renewable energy, as well as, create and support initiatives that strive to improve energy literacy and sustainability on campus.

name: Student Society for Sustainable Systems Engineers
summary: The Student Society for Sustainable Systems Engineers (S4E) is the dedicated club for students in the sustainable systems engineering major. We foster camaraderie, provide support and connect SUSE students with academic and career-based resources.

name: Student Veterinary Emergency & Critical Care Society
description: Our club aims to promote interest in veterinary emergency and critical care medicine by providing opportunities for hands on experiences in team environments, furthering education and exploring work opportunities in this exciting field. Membership restricted to current UCVM students.
summary: Our club aims to promote interest in veterinary emergency and critical care medicine by providing opportunities for hands on experiences in team environments, furthering education and exploring work opportunities in this exciting field.

name: Students for Direct Action
description: Students for Direct Action is a directly democratic club dedicated to empowering students with the skills necessary to participate in and foment movements that share our values surrounding the Labour Movement and Co-operative Economics, Social Democratic Reforms, Environmentalism, Social issues, and Human Rights. We aim to accomplish our campaign goals through peaceful direct action. Members campaign for a just world while practicing and learning skills such as creating activist art, canvassing, co-operative business development, policy and community development research, engaging in or creating media, public speaking at protests and events, logistics and event-planning, nonpartisan electoral politics, and much more! SDA's Five Values: The Labour Movement and Cooperative Economics - Our institutions are almost always undemocratic. We support unions and worker organization which provide workers with representation and the ability to address grievances, usually directly and with fellow workers. Cooperative economics is about direct worker ownership and governance of firms in the form of worker and multi-stakeholder cooperatives. This logic applies to all institutions, including universities. Social Democratic Reforms - We believe that healthcare, housing, clean water, a living wage, quality food, mental healthcare, and all basic needs are a human right. We support the defense and expansion of these services to benefit everyone. Environmentalism - We recognize that we are not separate from nature, and that environmental problems are social problems, and social problems are environmental problems. We recognize that we need to act rapidly to decolonize and avert climate change, as well as create resilient, locally governed and self-reliant communities. Social Issues - We know that racism, sexism, ableism, queerphobia, and more are pervasive in our institutions and culture, and we are committed to rooting out these forms of oppression from within ourselves and the wider community. Human Rights - The UN Declaration of Human Rights is explicit. Freedom of press, speech, assembly, freedom of association, freedom from danger and hunger, the right to democratic participation and more are inalienable. For these reasons, we oppose all international and domestic acts of military aggression, colonization, and genocide.
summary: SDA is a directly democratic club that employs grassroots organizing campaigns to build a just and sustainable world from the ground up. We empower students with the skills necessary to participate in and foment movements that share our 5 Values.

name: Students in Communications
description: We are communications students who are interested in developing a supportive community for our peers, to enhance the educational experience by providing a safe space for social, professional, and scholastic interaction. We strive to create a framework for students to find resources, network, advocate, and connect with peers and offer e vents, workshops, and community pl atforms to enhance academic activities with social elements in a relaxed and friendly atmosphere! To become a full member and gain access to exclusive events, perks, and office hours. Interested in joining the team? Check out our Executive application form! Stay tuned for the forms!
summary: SiC is creating a framework for communications students to find resources, network, have fun, advocate, and connect with peers. We offer events, workshops, and community platforms to enhance academic life with social elements in a relaxed atmosphere!

name: Students Interested in Medical Sciences
description: Students Interested in the Medical Sciences (SIMS) hosts events geared to promoting careers in medicine, medical research and the health care industry. We consist of undergraduate students who have developed curiosity or an interest in the field of medicine. The organization has a membership of over 100 members annually. Aside from hosting many events where we bring in physicians, directors and experts from different medical fields, we also have social outings, volunteer opportunities and organize fundraising events for charities or relief efforts.
summary: Students Interested in the Medical Sciences (SIMS) is an undergraduate club at the University of Calgary that hosts events geared to promoting careers in medicine, medical research and the health care industry.

name: Students of iGEM Association
description: Students of iGEM is currently rebranding to Chimera! Chimera is a student-led research club organizing hands-on, interdisciplinary projects with a focus in synthetic biology. We bring together students from science, engineering, arts and business to tackle real-world challenges through our research projects! Some current examples include converting campus waste into biofuels for renewable energy production on campus, and developing a blood substitute through genetic engineering with microbes. Whether you're passionate about research, entrepreneurship, sustainability, or innovative problem-solving, Chimera offers you the chance to collaborate with driven students, gain leadership experience, and turn ideas into impactful solutions. Members can participate in research teams, help us bring science to high schools, help us run small startups on campus to bootstrap and fundraise, help organize research symposiums and conferences to educate the public, attend our research and pitch skill building workshops, participate in our research competitions, or just enjoy fun activities and get to know passionate students! Join us and support cutting-edge science while building your skills and expanding your network! Find the application and discord for Chimera, and the 2025 iGEM team application, here !
summary: Chimera is a student-led research club promoting science on campus, providing students with research experience and entrepreneurship opportunities! We solve local issues through hands-on collaborative research projects, and host the UCalgary iGEM team.

name: Students of the Arts and Science Honours Academy
description: SASHA is the official representative body for all students who are a part of the Arts and Science Honours Academy at the University of Calgary. We host events and offer opportunities for involvement, socialization, academic enrichment, and networking.
summary: SASHA is the official representative body for all students who are a part of the Arts and Science Honours Academy at the University of Calgary. We host events and offer opportunities for involvement, socialization, academic enrichment, and networking.

name: Students Supporting Israel
description: Students Supporting Israel (SSI) is a dynamic and passionate student club that stands out for its unwavering dedication to promoting a positive understanding of Israel on campus. With a focus on education, cultural engagement, and collaboration, SSI provides a vibrant and inclusive community for students interested in Israel's rich history, culture, and contributions to the world. Education lies at the heart of SSI's mission. Recognizing that Israel is one of the most complex places in the world, the club places a strong emphasis on providing accurate and nuanced information. SSI organizes informative events, workshops, and discussions that delve into various aspects of Israeli society, politics, and history. By fostering a deeper understanding of the complexities surrounding Israel, SSI aims to equip students with the knowledge and tools necessary for constructive dialogue and engagement. SSI takes pride in its inclusive nature. It is not a religious club, welcoming members from diverse religious and cultural backgrounds who share a common interest in Israel. The club values diversity and actively encourages students from all walks of life to join and engage in open and respectful conversations. This diversity enriches the discussions and perspectives within the club, promoting a broader understanding of Israel's multifaceted identity. Collaboration and coalition building are essential pillars of SSI's approach. The club seeks to create meaningful partnerships and coalitions with other student organizations, both on campus and beyond. By working collaboratively with like-minded groups, SSI aims to amplify its impact and foster a broader coalition dedicated to promoting understanding, dialogue, and peace. This cooperative spirit allows SSI to engage with a wider audience and create a more significant and lasting impact. By fostering a deeper understanding of Israel's complexities, embracing diverse perspectives, and actively seeking partnerships, SSI continues to inspire and empower students to become advocates for a positive and nuanced portrayal of Israel on campus and in the wider community.
summary: Students Supporting Israel promotes positive understanding of Israel on campus through education, culture, and collaboration. SSI aims to be inclusive and diverse, fostering dialogue and nuanced advocacy.

name: Students' Union Clubs
description: Welcome to SU Clubs! This ClubHub portal is for SU Clubs Office staff only. For a list of clubs you can join, check out the full list here! Joining a club is one of the best ways to enhance your post-secondary experience. SU Registered Clubs are autonomous, student-run initiatives that improve student life at UCalgary. Registering your organization with the SU provides access to funding, services, and other benefits including tables at Clubs Week, lockers, meeting space, and more! SU Clubs are under the Vice President Internal's portfolio and day-to-day operations are managed by the Coordinators, Student Organizations. Check the Clubs Manual for a full list of rights and responsibilities of Registered Student Organizations!
summary: With over 300 registered clubs at the University of Calgary, your Students' Union is committed to supporting the multitude of interests and activities of undergraduate students. There's something for everyone!

name: Swing Dance Club, UofC
description: Welcome to Swing with UofC! We are an University of Calgary SU-sanctioned club with the goal of spreading our love of swing dancing around campus. No partner or experience is required, and everyone is welcome! Please check out our website for all the information: https://swingwithuofc.wix.com/ucsdc If you need to contact us for any reason, send an email to: swingwithuofc@gmail.com.
summary: Welcome to Swing with UofC! We are an University of Calgary SU-sanctioned club with the goal of spreading our love of swing dancing around campus. No partner or experience is required, and everyone is welcome!

name: Taiwanese Students' Association
description: Bringing together University of Calgary students who have a shared interest in Taiwanese culture, are of Taiwanese ethnicity who wish to connect with other like-individuals, or those who wish to make new friends in an inclusive environment!
summary: Bringing together University of Calgary students who have a shared interest in Taiwanese culture, are of Taiwanese ethnicity who wish to connect with other like-individuals, or those who wish to make new friends in an inclusive environment!

name: Taking Strides Calgary
description: Taking Strides is a student-run, non-profit organization, whose main priority is to benefit the lives of children with disabilities and special needs through an inclusive, weekly program designed to improve physical literacy and social interaction. We run adaptive sport, adaptive rock climbing, aquatics, teen and rhythm & strength programs. On top of this, we strive to promote student leadership and activism by providing a unique volunteer experience that will guide students in their future careers and journeys.
summary: Taking Strides is a student-run, non-profit organization, whose main priority is to benefit the lives of children with disabilities and special needs through inclusive, weekly sessions designed to improve physical literacy through a variety of programs.

name: Tech Start UCalgary
description: We take on interesting problems and use technology to solve them. Each year, we combine software, design, and strategy to build team software projects. At the end of the year, our teams present their finished projects to our sponsors at our annual showcase. We also explore the world of startups and tech entrepreneurship with various events throughout the year. Find out how to get involved and learn more at our website https://techstartucalgary.com !
summary: Each year, we combine software, design, and strategy to build collaborative software projects. We also explore the world of startups and tech entrepreneurship with various events throughout the year. Find out more at techstartucalgary.com!

name: Thai Student Society
description: Thai Student Society (TSS) is a student‑run hub that welcomes Thai undergrads—and anyone curious about Thailand—to connect, create, and celebrate together. Our purpose: Build a friendly network for Thai students away from home while inviting the broader campus to explore Thai culture. Provide a safe, inclusive space that encourages meaningful relationships, teamwork, and personal growth. How we do it: Monthly socials – relaxed gatherings featuring pot‑luck Thai dishes, board‑games, film nights, or tuk‑tuk‑speed ice‑breakers that help new faces feel instantly at home. Interactive workshops – pad‑thai masterclasses, slang & language crash courses, traditional dance and craft sessions, plus fusion art projects that blend Thai flair with global perspectives. Signature events – campus‑wide Thai Cultural Night, Songkran water‑festival, and Loy Krathong lantern‑float, often run in partnership with other cultural clubs to enrich UCalgary's multicultural scene. Support & outreach – mentorship circles, volunteer drives, and collaborations that amplify Thai voices and foster intercultural understanding across Calgary. Membership is free , open year‑round, and packed with chances to learn new skills, lead projects, and share the spirit of “ruam‑jai, ruam‑sanook”—together in heart, together in fun!
summary: Thai Student Society (TSS) unites Thai students & culture enthusiasts at UCalgary and across Calgary. We host monthly socials and flagship events like Songkran & Loy Krathong to build community, showcase Thai heritage, and spark intercultural exchange.

name: The Anxiety Awareness Club
description: The Anxiety Awareness Club is a club open to University of Calgary students and the general public. The purpose of this club is to raise awareness about anxiety through various methods such as brochures, posters, events, and through an online presence in social media. The goal in mind with these is to reduce stigmas surrounding anxiety, making those who suffer from anxiety to any extent feel accepted rather than being judged. This club's purpose also includes events and informative sessions whether online or in person that can help individuals suffering from anxiety to learn techniques that can help deal with their anxiety, while also providing a safe space where people who suffer from anxiety or people who wish to help those suffering from anxiety can go to express their ideas and concerns. Further, the club will raise funds for organizations that promote the well being of individuals who deal with anxiety as well as help spread awareness about anxiety.
summary: The Anxiety Awareness Club is a club open to University of Calgary students, with the purpose of raising awareness and reducing stigmas related to anxiety. We promise to help provide students with techniques/safe spaces to help deal with anxiety.

name: The Children's Health Advocates in support of the Alberta Children's Hospital Foundation
description: Mission The Children's Health Advocates (CHA) aims to promote the importance of collaborative efforts in healthcare by fostering engagement between university students and community organizations such as the Alberta Children's Hospital Foundation (ACHF), Children's Cottage Society (CCS), CP Kids & Families, and Brown Bagging for Calgary's Kids (BB4CK). Description As the 2024–2025 SU Club of the Year , the Children's Health Advocates is a student-led organization committed to raising funds and encouraging participation in initiatives that support child health and wellbeing. Each year, over 94,000 children and families rely on the Alberta Children's Hospital for specialized care. CHA proudly partners with the Alberta Children's Hospital Foundation to advocate for excellence in pediatric health, research, and family-centered care—funding life-saving equipment, enhanced treatments, and innovative research. We are recognized by the Foundation as a group of passionate student advocates dedicated to advancing its mission. Beyond our hospital-centered efforts, CHA is also devoted to sparking critical conversations on pediatric care, organizing volunteer opportunities, and leading fundraising events that support organizations such as BB4CK, the Alberta Red Cross, and the University of Calgary Child Care Centre. Through strategic partnerships with other student clubs, we strive to host engaging, meaningful events that create a positive impact both on campus and in the wider community. Follow our journey and get involved by checking us out on Instagram: @uofccha
summary: The Children's Health Advocates is dedicated to fundraising and advocating for the Alberta Children's Hospital Foundation and other children's advocacy organizations.

name: The Cybersecurity Club
description: Formerly the The Information Security Club (InfoSec, 2019 - 2024). The Cybersecurity Club focuses on helping students explore and develop information security and cybersecurity-related skills. To join our club, please start by joining our Discord server.  All communication, announcements, and meetings are organized here : https://discord.gg/wVwv5Pw
summary: Dedicated to providing space for students to learn information security tools and skills.  Hosting hands on workshops, speakers on various infosec topics, and capture the flag hacking competitions.  Open to students of all background and faculties!

name: The English Club
description: The English Club provides a space for students and community members to engage with English literature and media. We aim to foster connections and meaningful relationships through discussions, events, and a shared passion for English.
summary: The English Club provides a space for students and community members to engage with English literature and media. We aim to foster connections and meaningful relationships through discussions, events, and a shared passion for English.

name: The Fine Arts Society
summary: The Fine Arts Society hosts events that allow artists of different backgrounds and experience to engage in practices of making and learning. We host workshops, open studio work sessions, and gallery walks.

name: The Jazz Room
summary: TJR is dedicated to providing students of all backgrounds and abilities with opportunities to appreciate and enjoy jazz. Our club will offer spaces to listen to and play jazz music, as well as fundraise and support jazz.

name: The Mustard Seed, U of C
description: The Mustard Seed U of C is an on-campus club representing The Mustard Seed. Working together, we strive to accomplish our shared vision of "Building community, growing hope, and supporting change." The aim of our club is to combat poverty at its root by supporting the goals of The Mustard Seed - meeting the basic needs of shelter, food, and clothing. We encourage and provide acceptance to those experiencing poverty and strive to help those in need find sustainable housing and employment. We achieve these goals through on-campus fundraisers and advocacy events, as well as through off-campus volunteering opportunities such as meal-preparation and donation sorting at the Mustard Seed shelter. Join us today to help combat poverty in Calgary! CONTACT INFORMATION: Gmail - theseeduofc@gmail.com. Facebook - www.facebook.com/groups/theseeduofc/. Instagram - theseeduofc
summary: The Mustard Seed U of C is an on-campus club representing The Mustard Seed. Working together, we strive to accomplish our shared vision of "Building community, growing hope, and supporting change." The aim of our club is to combat poverty at its root b...

name: The Production Animal Health Club
description: The Production Animal Health Club will be comprised of veterinary medical students interested in the health of food and fibre producing animals. The emphasis of the club will be focused on developing skills in monitoring the health of animals on a population and individual-animal basis, understanding regulatory issues associated with animal production, and economic aspects of animal production industries. The club will organize and participate in learning activities involving 1) Seminars 2) Work experience 3) Day trips 4) Fundraising events 5) Volunteer activities 6) Conferences and more. Suggested experts involved in these activities will include veterinarians, regulatory agencies, industry experts, Department of Agriculture and Rural Development of the Government of Alberta and the Canadian Food Inspection Agency
summary: The Production Animal Health Club will be comprised of students interested in the health of food and fibre producing animals. The emphasis of the club will be focused on developing skills in monitoring the health of food and fibre-producing animals on ...

name: The Sandbox
description: This is an example of a portal intended for use by SU Staff and Ambassadors to Student Organizations. We call it "The Sandbox" because it's here for training purposes. Build, play, and explore the features of ClubHub so that you're better able to train your peers on our comprehensive Clubs portal system.
summary: This is an example of a portal intended for use by SU Staff. We call it "The Sandbox" because it's here for training purposes. Build, play, and explore the features of ClubHub so that you're better able to train..

name: Turkish Arts & Peace Collective
description: The TAP Collective is a student-led initiative at the University of Calgary that uses art and cultural expression as tools for peacebuilding, dialogue, and community connection. Rooted in Turkish traditions but open to all, TAP creates inclusive spaces where students from diverse backgrounds can engage in creative workshops, music sessions, dialogue circles, and collaborative projects. Through hands-on experiences and meaningful conversations, TAP promotes mental well-being, intercultural understanding, and leadership development. The collective empowers students to share their voices, celebrate diversity, and build bridges across cultures — enriching campus life with creativity, compassion, and community spirit.
summary: A UCalgary club using Turkish arts to build community, foster inclusion, and promote peace through creative workshops, dialogue, and cultural exchange open to students of all backgrounds.

name: U of C Badminton Club
description: UCBC is a place for our members to take a break from academics to stay active and meet new people. All skill levels welcome! For any questions, please feel free to reach out to us via email: uofcalgarybc2@gmail.com or Instagram: uofcbadmintonclub
summary: The Badminton Club is formed to promote an active living lifestyle. Our goals are to encourage more people to play badminton and create a network where people can socialize with each other.

name: U of C Creative Writing Circle
description: The U of C Creative Writing Circle (CWC) is an organization which endeavours to create a positive, supportive, and inspirational community for students wanting to express themselves through all forms of creative writing, regardless of their experience. Visit our LinkTree page to learn more about us: https://linktr.ee/UofC_CWC.
summary: The U of C Creative Writing Circle (CWC) is an organization which endeavours to create a positive, supportive, and inspirational community for students wanting to express themselves through all forms of creative writing, regardless of their experience.

name: U of C Film Society
description: The University of Calgary Film Society is an organization formed by enthusiastic students and film viewers who enjoy learning the art, technique and history of cinema. We are a community dedicated to the entertainment and inspiration of our members through our regular screenings of influential and quality films, as well as our various film-related events. The U of C Film Society offers its members the opportunity to view and discuss films, as well as the possibility to meet new and exciting people who share an interest in films. We are a non-profit organization, composed of members who pay an annual membership fee of five dollars. Our programs are structured in a way that provides for our member's various interests and tailors screenings towards many different tastes. We believe in the power, ingenuity and beauty of film as one of the most potent mediums for expression. The aim of the University of Calgary Film Society is to foster an understanding and appreciation of films. We endeavor to provide our members with exceptional and quality film viewing experiences, as well as opportunities for members to partake in engaging and interesting events throughout the year. Our screenings are designed to inspire, enlighten and open the minds of students to the broader, wider horizons that cinema offers.
summary: The University of Calgary Film Society is an organization formed by enthusiastic students and film viewers who enjoy learning the art, technique and history of cinema.

name: U of C Ski and Board Club
description: The U of C Ski and Board Club is a student run non-profit organization that is dedicated to the promotion of skiing and snowboarding to the students of the U of C. The Ski and Board Club is the largest, oldest (and best) club on campus. We have been around for over 60 years now! Some of you may be thinking that we're off our rockers if you know that the U of C isn't even 60 years old, but I promise you, we DO know how to count. The U of C Ski and Board Club was formed in 1962 when the University Of Calgary was still operating as a branch of the University of Alberta. When the U of C was established in 1966 the Calgary branch of the then U of A Ski Club became the U of C Ski Club you know and love! ** Please note, we do not manage our memberships nor accept membership requests through club hub, we will be selling memberships during clubs week September 16th, 17th, 18th, 2024 at booth 68!! If you miss us then come find us at our office hours M-Th 11am-2pm at the tables near the Jugo Juice unless otherwise posted in our instagram!**
summary: The U of C Ski and Board Club is a student run non-profit organization that is dedicated to the promotion of skiing and snowboarding to the students of the U of C. The Ski and Board Club is the largest, oldest (and best) club on campus. We been around ...

name: UC Cheer
description: The University of Calgary Cheer Team is a group of university students who support their school, community and the sport of cheerleading in Calgary. Their first season started in January of 2012 where the team performed at the Dino Men's Basketball Games. The team continues to perform at football and basketball games and various events around the city, as well as competing at competitions across Canada.
summary: The University of Calgary Cheer Team is a group of university students who support their school, community and the sport of cheerleading in Calgary. Their first season started in January of 2012 where the team performed at the Dino Men's Basketball Gam...

name: UC Dance Company
description: The UC Dance Company is a team composed of elite dancers within the University of Calgary who aim to support the school and community. Founded in 2019, the team performs at varsity athletic games and events, and engages in charitable functions around Calgary. With weekly practices, the team will work on choreography for performances, technique, fitness, and flexibility, while building an atmosphere of trust and a sense of community. Please contact us through Instagram about membership.
summary: The UC Dance Company is a team composed of high level dancers within the University of Calgary who aim to support the community performing at university athletic games and charitable events while working on choreography, technique, and team spirit.

name: UC Moot Court Society
description: The UCMCS aims to provide a competitive avenue for undergraduate students interested in the law, via intercollegiate moot court and mock trial competitions held nationwide. The UCMCS' key objective is to develop the public speaking and critical thinking skills of a dedicated team of students committed to the art of legal advocacy, as well as introduce undergraduate students to the competitive world of moot court.
summary: Moot court is a trial simulation in which competitors appeal a trial decision to Supreme Court judges; our organization trains and funds students to compete in nmooting competitions.

name: UC University Bible Fellowship
description: Calgary UBF is a Christian organization and part of an international Christian organization named UBF. We have branch chapters on many campuses all over the world. Our objectives are helping students to find the direction and meaning of life in Jesus Christ, grow as future national Christian leaders at the University of Calgary, City of Calgary, Canada, and all over the world. We provide leadership activities for Christian students, foster fellowship, and friendship among the students through the weekly meetings, seasonal retreats, conferences, or volunteer work. Students have leadership and volunteer work opportunities abroad through the numerous branch chapters in 6 continents. We do networking with other clubs and organizations on campus for the university community, and actively do humanitarian work for the less fortunate to share the love of Christ.
summary: Calgary UBF is a Christian organization that helps the University of Calgary students study the Bible through individual and group Bible study and have a personal faith in Jesus as his/her Saviour. We do leadership activities and humanitarian work.

name: UCalgary Bhangra Club
description: UCBC focuses on promoting student wellness in an enthusiastic and energetic way. Through our Bhangra workshops we hope to create a space which students are able to use as a stress-reliever. Our goal will be to keep these workshops positivity-filled, entertaining, and a break from one's daily routine. Our members and volunteers are able to help lead and keep these workshops organized and well-run. Our members and volunteers will also be able to contribute with other club events that promote Bhangra and its origins such as various forms of fundraisers. We aim to be a club that encourages students to focus on their mental health as well as physical health and shine light on the importance of taking well needed breaks. What better way to do that than through a heart-pumping dance routine?
summary: UCBC focuses on promoting student wellness in an enthusiastic and energetic way. Join our Bhangra workshops to relieve some stress by learning an exciting and heart-pumping dance routine. Volunteer with us to learn more about Bhangra and its origins!

name: UCalgary Fashion Network
description: The UCalgary Fashion Network (UCFN) is a student-run network that provides an opportunity for individuals to raise awareness about diversity and sustainability in the fashion industry, engage in community development projects, connect with fashion enterprises, and network with leaders in the industry. At its most basic, we see fashion as a natural intersection of art, individuality, culture, and history, and we believe that everyone should have to opportunity to express themselves and their identities through it in a manner that is sustainable and inclusive. Our student organization offers its members the opportunity to connect with like minded peers, network with fashion industry professionals, give back to their community, and engage in a variety of club events, including group workshops, leadership seminars, movie and trivia nights, and more.
summary: The UCalgary Fashion Network is a student-run network providing opportunities for individuals to raise awareness about diversity and sustainability in the fashion industry, engage in community development projects, and network with industry professionals

name: UCalgary Parks Canada Club
description: The purpose of this club is to: • Inspire community members to get outdoors, have fun, promote exploration of Canada's parks and make it accessible to all • Promote volunteering and employment opportunities at Parks Canada for students at the University of Calgary, • Bring the community together to appreciate and protect Canada's natural and cultural heritage and ensure they are committed to conserving this heritage. Each year, the club will be participating in conservation work and outdoor activities that are inclusive of all skill-levels and abilities. The Parks Canada Club will also be experimentally exploring Canada's natural and cultural heritage. This club is a part of a network of Parks Canada Campus Clubs across the country.
summary: The purpose of this club is to inspire community members to get outdoors, have fun, promote exploration of Canada's parks, and provide volunteering and employment opportunities at Parks Canada. We aim to make this accessible to all.

name: UCalgary Sociology and Law & Society Association
description: Our objectives: To bring together University of Calgary undergraduate students within the Sociology and Law and Society programs ; To provide University of Calgary undergraduate students with information and opportunities for development within their undergraduate studies ; To engage students in academic and occupational network building opportunities wherein students may develop competitive networking and communication skills conducive to their respective fields of study ; To retain channels of communication between University of Calgary departmental staff and undergraduate students ; To represent University of Calgary undergraduate students with a vested interest in Sociology and Law and Society within the various channels of communication at the university; To support University of Calgary undergraduate students to develop an understanding of their interests and how that may be translated in the competitive job market.
summary: The UCalgary Sociology and Law & Society Association is a student-led organization that is dedicated to enhancing the undergraduate student experience through social events, networking and career opportunities, as well as student advocacy.

name: UCalgary Toastmasters
description: We are the UCalgary chapter of Toastmasters, a nonprofit educational organization that builds confidence and teaches public speaking and leadership skills through a worldwide network of clubs. In a supportive community environment, members can prepare and deliver speeches, respond to impromptu questions, and give and receive constructive feedback. During each meeting, there are various designated roles to keep members engaged. Besides the main speakers who prepare the delivered speeches and engage in impromptu speaking, meeting roles include the Grammarian, who introduces the word of the day, the Ahh-counter, who watches for filler words and pauses, the Table Topics Master, who prepares the impromptu questions, and the timer, to name a few.
summary: We are the UCalgary chapter of Toastmasters, a nonprofit organization that builds confidence and teaches public speaking skills. In a supportive community, members deliver speeches, respond to impromptu questions, and give/receive constructive feedback.

name: UCVM Equine Club
description: The Equine Club is a club that is geared to increase the exposure of vet med students to the equine industry. We aim to do this through zoom talks, seminars, videos, wet labs, weekend horse clinics, farm tours and trips and potentially international travel. Throughout the years, each event has been organized to engage student learning in equine health and medicine to expand their knowledge and competence as future veterinarians assisting horses and owners. We are also a student chapter of the American Association of Equine Practitioners (AAEP) who provide funding for events and trips, opportunities to go to conferences, and scholarships.
summary: The Equine Club is a club that is geared to increase the exposure of vet med students to the equine industry. We aim to do this through seminars, videos, wet labs, weekend horse clinics, farm tours and trips and potentially international travel. This u...

name: UCVM Investigative Medicine
description: The IM club exists to provide a medium to bring together veterinary students, veterinarians, graduate students, and researchers to foster an environment with a common objective of advancing education and collaboration within veterinary research.
summary: The IM club exists to provide a medium to bring together veterinary students, veterinarians, graduate students, and researchers to foster an environment with a common objective of advancing education and collaboration within veterinary research.

name: UCVM Pathology Club
description: This is a club for the veterinary pathology enthusiasts of UCVM. Our aim is to provide extra experience in veterinary pathology through field experience and seminars, as well as help students network and connect with current veterinary pathologists.
summary: This is a club for the veterinary pathology enthusiasts of UCVM. Our aim is to provide extra experience in veterinary pathology through field experience and seminars, as well as help students network and connect with current veterinary pathologists.

name: UCVM Radiology Club
description: The Rad club is a student-run organization specifically for students enrolled in the faculty of Veterinary Medicine at the University of Calgary. The clubs purpose is to aid students in learning Veterinary Radiology as a discipline including ultrasound, radiographs, computerized tomography, and magnetic resonating imagery. We organize events based in equine, food animal, exotics and small animal medicine in order to build on and complement skills taught in the Doctor of Veterinary Medicine program.
summary: The RadVet club is a student-run organization specifically for students enrolled in the faculty of Veterinary Medicine at the University of Calgary. The clubs purpose is to aid students in learning Veterinary Radiology as a discipline.

name: Uddering Engineers Podcast
description: A podcast created by engineering students for engineering students at the University of Calgary. Uddering Engineers focuses on providing students with advice from a student perspective about how to navigate the university experience, how to leverage clubs, organizations, and university resources for the best experience and most rewarding engineering experience. This podcast focuses on advocating about issues concerning the student body whether it be about mental health, the next steps in your career, diversity, and inclusion, etc.
summary: o

name: Undergraduate Nursing Society
description: The University of Calgary's Undergraduate Nursing Society (UNS) is a student-led organization maintained by nursing students, for nursing students! UNS endeavours to enrich the lives of nursing students professionally, academically, and socially through a variety of initiatives including volunteerism, fundraisers, mentorship opportunities, advocacy, and other fun events. The UNS plays a vital role in communication to nursing students across all years of the undergraduate program and aims to support students in being and doing their best over the course of their nursing education.
summary: University of Calgary's Undergraduate Nursing Society is a club maintained by Nursing students for Nursing students! We work hard to raise nursing awareness and create a memorable experience for our students during their 4 year journey through the prog...

name: UNICEF on Campus
description: UNICEF on Campus is a non-profit club, open to anyone interested in playing a role to provide children the support and resources they deserve. Whether you just want to meet others, or are fully committed to regular involvement within the club, we welcome you to join us in raising awareness! If you are interested in joining our club please contact unicefucalgary@gmail.com as membership requests through Engage do not guarantee membership with our club.
summary: UNICEF on Campus is a non-profit club, open to anyone interested in playing a role to provide children the support and resources they deserve. Please email us at unicefucalgary@gmail.com for any membership inquiries.

name: University of Calgary Campus Conservative Association
description: Our club is designed to be a pipeline for young people interested in conservative politics to get involved in the Conservative family both federally and provincially in Alberta. We help facilitate a connection between youth and the Conservative message of freedom, hope, and prosperity. Check out our social media to stay up-to-date with our events!
summary:
Our club is designed to get students involved in the Conservative family both federally and provincially. We help facilitate a connection between youth and the Conservative message of freedom, hope, and prosperity.

Be sure to check out our social media

name: University of Calgary Chess Club
description: The UCCC is geared towards promoting the sport of chess among all demographics, from beginner to master. Our objective is to assist fellow enthusiasts and players to hone their chess skills and continue to reach new peaks of excellence.
summary: The UCCC is geared towards promoting the sport of chess among all demographics, from beginner to master. Our objective is to assist fellow enthusiasts and players to hone their chess skills and continue to reach new peaks of excellence.

name: University of Calgary Consulting Association
description: The University of Calgary Consulting Association (UCCA), formerly Haskayne Consulting Club, strives to provide valuable opportunities for members to learn about and gain experience in a career in consulting. Our members are able to participate in pro-bono consulting engagements and internships with local businesses and non-profit organizations, allowing them to apply classroom knowledge. The UCCA's valued clients benefit from sound recommendations and deliverables supported with in-depth analysis and research that is valuable to their future goals. The UCCA provides networking opportunities with various consulting firms ranging in size through case interview workshops, events, and competitions.
summary: The University of Calgary Consulting Association (UCCA), formerly Haskayne Consulting Club, provides opportunities for members to learn about a career in consulting through events, unpaid internships, and pro-bono consulting projects.

name: University of Calgary Dental Society
description: The UCDS is a club dedicated to creating a network of people interested in the field of dentistry. The UCDS holds events to help future dentists (and people who are just curious) get more information about dentistry and its specializations, tips for applications to dental school, and seminars about the Dental Aptitude Test (DAT). We also aim to connect students with current dental students to expand their knowledge of the dental field. Join our mailing list below: https://forms.gle/kXye51m12ABWAXQv5
summary: The UCDS is a club dedicated to creating a network of people interested in the field of dentistry. The UCDS holds events to help future dentists (and people who are just curious) get more information about the discipline and dental school.

name: University of Calgary Fencing Club
summary: The University of Calgary Fencing program is one of the oldest sport fencing organizations in Calgary. We are open to people of all skill levels, offering foil, sabre, and epee in both dry and electric.

name: University of Calgary Firearms Association
description: The University of Calgary Firearms Association is dedicated to training its members in the safe operation, storage, transportation and use of firearms. It is also a discussion and social group for target shooters, hunters and even firearms enthusiasts to meet. The association will also provide opportunities to learn more about firearms, and operate them at fully licensed ranges outside of campus. As these events will stress safety as a top priority, and violations of safety in the presence of firearms/ammunition off campus will be met with severe and immediate as specified in our Constitution. The University of Calgary Firearms Association acknowledges the diverse and storied history of firearms tradition in Canada. While the Canadian Constitution does not guarantee the private citizen the right to bear arms, Canada's laws allow citizens to legally acquire non-restricted and restricted firearms with proper licensing. The association will strongly promote the benefits of this legal private firearms ownership as hobbyists, hunters and general enthusiasts. The association will represent our interests as firearms owners and enthusiasts while attempting to dispel misconceptions about firearms and their owners.
summary: The University of Calgary Firearms Association is dedicated to training its members in the safe operation, storage, transportation and use of firearms.

name: University of Calgary Kiva Chapter
description: UCalgary Kiva is a team of University of Calgary undergraduate students from a diverse set of backgrounds in finance, technology, medicine, and much more. We are driven by a united passion for positive impact to utilize our skill sets to help underserved communities thrive. As a Kiva chapter, we raise funds to lend to Kiva's borrowers and promote Kiva's mission—to provide financial freedom to everyone in the world through microfinance. In doing so, we support sustainable economic development in communities with limited economic opportunities, one microloan at a time.
summary: UCalgary Kiva is the first and only student microfinance group at UCalgary. Our organization is a chapter of Kiva.org, a non-profit institution that helps provide life-changing microloans to people in need worldwide, especially in developing nations.

name: University of Calgary New Democrats
description: We are your campus club for the New Democratic Party, here, at the University of Calgary. We are a group of open minded, progressive, and social democratic individuals who are dedicated to promoting social justice, environmentalism, and the greater welfare of all Canadians. As the UCNDP, we are here to serve as a resource for members of the University, interested in current social democratic issues and help expand and grow the New Democratic Party and their philosophies to the campus community.
summary: We are your campus club for the New Democratic Party! We exist to connect UofC students to progressive initiatives and the New Democratic Party.

name: University of Calgary Trivia Club
description: Who invented the telephone? In what year did the Titanic sink? What is a group of flamingos called? University of Calgary Trivia Club (UCTC) is a casual and semi-competitive club that caters to all those who enjoy trivia no matter how trivial! Whether you're a trivia enthusiast or just enjoy learning fun facts, UCTC provides a welcoming space to challenge your knowledge, meet like-minded peers, and engage in exciting trivia events.
summary: Who invented the telephone? In what year did the Titanic sink? What is a group of flamingos called? University of Calgary Trivia Club (UCTC) is a casual and semi-competitive club that caters to all those who enjoy trivia no matter how trivial!

name: UofC Fibre Arts Club
description: The UofC Fibre Arts Club welcomes everyone who is interested in fibre arts at the University of Calgary. Ranging from knitting, crocheting, felting, weaving, and more. Our club is a place to share your artistry and connect with fellow fibre arts enthusiasts. Our aim is to create a community where happiness is transmitted and joy is shared for everything related to fibre arts. Whether you are a beginner, or an expert, join us to dive further into the marvellous world of fibre arts!
summary: This ub that offers students the chance to unwind while doing different kinds of fibre art (knitting, crocheting, felting, weaving and embroidery)!

name: UofC FinTech
description: Welcome to UofC FinTech! We are a strategic student-led organization that bridges the gap between students and the global fintech industry. Through its official international partnerships, UofC FinTech serves as the exclusive regional representative and exhibitor agent for global fintech conferences. The organization not only provides students with direct access to international networking and business development opportunities, but also represents Calgary's fintech ecosystem on a global stage. UofC FinTech now operates at the intersection of student innovation, industry engagement, and international exposure offering real-world experience in business development, partnership management, and event execution. By leveraging partnerships, professional outreach, and institutional support, the organization aims to accelerate fintech education, entrepreneurship, and collaboration among the next generation of leaders.
summary: UofC FinTech is a dynamic community passionate about the convergence of technology and finance. This club aims to facilitate networking and collaboration in the fintech space. It will play a crucial role to promote the growth of fintech Initiatives.

name: UofC HOSA- Future Health Professionals
summary: HOSA is an organization that encourages health related competencies, education, and leadership to enhance the knowledge and skills of both the students and community. The most prominent event is the national and international conferences held annually.

name: UofC Poetry Club
description: We are a club bringing together UofC students who enjoy poetry in all forms. Our goal is to promote poetry as an accessible art form to members and non-members alike, whether new to poetry or a practiced poet. We establish spaces online and in-person where students have the opportunity to read, write and share poetry. Additionally, we collaborate with other poetry and writing organizations to strengthen our community. We yearn for a more poetic tomorrow!
