summary: We are a club bringing together poetry lovers at the U of C. All poetry readers and writers welcome, whether you're new to the art form or an experienced writer. Join our club to learn, grow your art, and support others like you!

name: UofC Pre-Law Society
description: The University of Calgary Pre-Law Society (UofCPLS) is where future lawyers find their footing. Since 2007, we have been the only SU-recognized club dedicated to law school hopefuls—backed by the faculty of law itself. From LSAT prep and admission panels to networking with practicing lawyers, we cut through the noise and provide students real tools, connections, and a genuine community. Open to all, no experience required—just curiosity and drive. Check out our Linktree !
summary: Est. 2007 | The University of Calgary Pre-Law Society is a community of like-minded individuals who aspire to pursue a career in the legal field; proudly supported by the UCalgary Faculty of Law.

name: UofC Relay for Life
description: UofC Relay for Life aims to provide a safe, entertaining, collaborative, and supportive environment to raise money for cancer research. It allows students to celebrate survivors and remember their loved ones. The annual fundraiser allows passionate students and community members to come together and join the fight against cancer.
summary: UofC Relay for Life aims to provide a safe, collaborative, supportive and fun environment for students to celebrate survivors and remember their loved ones while raising money for cancer research.

name: Uplift
summary: Uplift aims to support the academic growth of undergraduate students at the University of Calgary through the launch of a network of free group tutoring sessions. It also hopes to promote the development of business skills in Calgary's underserved youth.

name: Urban Calgary Students' Association
description: The objectives of UrbanCSA are to engage students on urban & architectural issues (especially those faced by Calgarians); and to bridge the gap between the student, the citizen, and the professional. UrbanCSA runs monthly speaker sessions, tours, academic workshops and more casual events. We also partner with different clubs on campus to provide our members with a multi-disciplinary and diverse experience. To stay updated sign up for our newsletter in our Lintree
summary: The objectives of UrbanCSA are to engage students on urban & architectural issues (especially those faced by Calgarians); and to bridge the gap between the student, the citizen, and the professional. Visit our linktree to stay updated! Linktr.ee/urbancsa

name: User Experience Design Club
description: The UX Design Club is a student-led space where creativity meets usability. We help students explore the world of user experience and interface design through hands-on workshops, collaborative projects, and community events. Whether you're just curious or already building your portfolio, our goal is to make design accessible, beginner-friendly, and fun while also helping you gain real-world skills, meet like-minded people, and grow your confidence in tech and design.
summary: A student club focused on UX and UI design. We host workshops, design jams, and socials to help students build skills, connect with others, and explore real-world design in a supportive, beginner-friendly space.

name: Vegan Club
description: The University of Calgary Vegan Club is a student-led community dedicated to promoting a compassionate, sustainable, and health-conscious lifestyle. We welcome vegans, vegetarians, and veg-curious individuals alike. Our mission is to raise awareness about animal welfare, environmental sustainability, and plant-based nutrition. Through educational events, social gatherings, and advocacy campaigns, we support students in making informed, ethical choices. The club is open to everyone interested in learning, sharing, and building a more compassionate campus. Instagram: @ucalgaryvegan Discord: https://discord.gg/499uvGMCYq
summary: To create community between vegans, vegetarians and veg-curious students on campus, to raise awareness about a plant-based lifestyle and to create a safe space for everyone.

name: Verbatim, the Undergraduate Linguistics Club
description: Verbatim is a club for students of linguistics and any student interested in language studies. Please come join us in CHC 209 for homework help, fun hangouts and a textbook library!
summary: Verbatim is a club for students of linguistics and any student interested in language studies. Please come join us in CHC 209 for homework help, fun hangouts and a textbook library!

name: Veterinary Business and Networking Club
description: The Veterinary Business and Networking Club (VBNC) is a new club who's mission is to introduce business aspects of the veterinary world to both future veterinarians and future veterinary industry leaders, whether these leaders are veterinarians or not, everyone is welcome. We have envisioned a club that seeks speakers and presenters that will discuss business and leadership topics that are not extensively covered in our Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree. This will not only improve club members understanding of the industry of Veterinary Medicine but the club will allow the members to increase the level of care that the veterinary industry will be providing its future patients and clients through initiatives and events created by the club.
summary: A club for all business and management savvy individuals. Our goal is to create an environment where future veterinary leaders can learn from current veterinary industry leaders about things that our curriculum does not cover.

name: Vietnamese Students' Association
description: The mission of the Vietnamese Students Association (VSA) at the University of Calgary is to establish a foundation where students can interact with, learn from, and inspire one another. This is accomplished by supporting the students' academic careers, developing strong social networks, promoting community involvement, and broadening the students' perspective on the Vietnamese experience through culture, heritage and language. Through the synergy and passion of all its members, VSA will strive towards and be able to attain this goal.
summary: The mission of the Vietnamese Students Association (VSA) at the University of Calgary is to establish a foundation where students can interact with, learn from, and inspire one another.

name: Wander Women
description: UCWaW is a club dedicated to empowerment in physical activity and the outdoors, with particular focus on women's empowerment. Our mission is to foster a sense of community through inclusion, representation and accessibility. We strive to encourage lifelong wellness through physical and mental health, and through lasting friendships. We welcome everyone comfortable in women-centred spaces and/or identifies as an ally that supports our club's mission and goals.
summary: UCWaW is dedicated to empowerment in fitness and the outdoors. Our goal is to foster community through accessibility, inclusion and representation. We welcome all women, femme, non-binary and allies comfortable in women centred spaces.

name: Wear Red Canada Club
summary: Wear Red Canada Club is powered by the Canadian Women's Heart Health Alliance and aims to raise awareness about women's heart health by organizing events, presentations, and collaborations. Join us to make a difference in Canadian women's heart outcomes!

name: Wellness Inclusion Diversity Equity Veterinary Student Club
description: The WIDE Club is a veterinary student group dedicated to providing a safe space for UCVM Black, Indigenous, People of Colour (BIPOC), LBGTQ and allies to come together in solidarity and to shine a much needed light on racism, exclusion and discrimination within the veterinary field. The aim of this group is to increase equity and inclusivity for all regardless of race, gender identity, sex, age, religion, family status or disability. The club is dedicated to providing information and resources regarding proactive anti-racism and anti-discriminatory practices, as well as materials related to mental health and professional development.
summary: We are a veterinary student group dedicated to promoting mental health, as well as creating a safe space for BIPOC, LBGTQ, & ally students by increasing awareness of anti-racism and anti-discriminatory practices within UCVM.

name: Wildlife Conservation Club
description: The Wildlife Conservation Club (WCC) (Formerly WWF-Calgary) strives to create awareness for the ongoing issues of endangered species at risk of extinction. The club also focuses on promoting the importance of conserving the natural habitat of species at risk. The club shall serve to fundraise money towards conservation projects for WCC, as well as to provide volunteering opportunities for students on campus to be involved in the community, such as volunteering in shoreline cleanups and tree-planting events.
summary: The WCC aims to increase awareness regarding wildlife that are at risk of extinction through different volunteering opportunities and fundraising events in which the funds will be donated to chosen wildlife conservation organizations.

name: Wings of Wisdom
description: Wings of Wisdom is a student-led club founded at the University of Calgary showcasing diverse healthcare careers beyond med school. From public health to biotech, WOW empowers students to discover their wings in healthcare and leave them saying WOW!
summary: Wings of Wisdom is a student-led club founded at the University of Calgary showcasing diverse healthcare careers beyond med school. From public health to biotech, WOW empowers students to discover their wings in healthcare and leave them saying WOW!

name: Women in Business
description: Women in Business is a student-run organization that aims to empower women pursuing careers in business and leadership roles within those careers, and to bring awareness to on-going pertinent workplace gender issues. We are focused on developing students' general knowledge about the issue, personal and professional networks, and professional skills, so as to provide a strong launchpad for their careers!
summary: Women in Business is a student-run organization that aims to empower women pursuing careers in business and leadership roles within those careers, and to bring awareness to on-going pertinent workplace gender issues.

name: Women in Computer Science
description: We are a club focused on and dedicated to increasing the visibility of and fostering a community for women and other minorities in Computer Science. In such a male-dominated environment, we are creating an opportunity for women to bond with like-minded women in the same field, and to continue to cultivate their programming, networking, and career skills. We are also a space for women in any major or from any background with little to no coding experience to come if they would like to learn about the wonderful world of programming but are not quite sure where to start.
summary: A place for women in computer science to come together, learn together, grow together, and succeed together. We want to help bridge the gap of female representation in computer science fields.

name: Women in Cybersecurity
description: The cybersecurity sector is predominantly male-dominated, highlighting a lack of representation for women and non-binary individuals rather than an excess of men. The University of Calgary lacks clubs specifically encouraging women and non-binary students to join the cybersecurity field. Addressing this gap, an official WiCyS chapter at the university aims to equip women and non-binary students with tools for success in cybersecurity. This includes fostering collaboration, networking, mentorship, and social interaction with peers in the field, both nationally and internationally. The club also focuses on facilitating employment opportunities through work-integrated learning and specialized certifications. Partnering with ICTC-CTIC, the club is part of a Cybersecurity Training and Work Integrated Learning Program supported by Microsoft, the Government of Canada, and WiCyS.
summary: Women in Cybersecurity aims to connect female and non-binary students at the University of Calgary to resources such as employers, mentors and cybersecurity certifications. The organization is a registered student chapter under the WiCyS global network.

name: Women In Politics
description: Women in Politics is aims to foster an inclusive, equitable, safe space within the realm of politics. We are committed to promoting diversity of thought, embracing all perspectives, and empowering individuals from all backgrounds and gender identites to participate meaningfully in political discourse. Through education, advocacy, and community engagement, we strive to dismantle barriers to inclusion and cultivate an environment where every voice is heard and respected. Together, we work towards a more just and representative political landscape, where equity and fairness guide our actions and decisions
summary: Aiming to provide a safe and social space for students in political science at the University of Calgary.

name: Women in Science and Engineering
description: Women in Science and Engineering (WISE) is a University of Calgary student organization dedicated to supporting and encouraging women who are interested or engaged in science or engineering careers. WISE works towards fostering an academic environment where both female and male students can promote the participation of women in science and engineering. Established in 1990, WISE provides a support system for students through course reviews, educational tutorials, guest speakers, field trips, networking, student mixer events, speaker series, professional development workshops, mentorship, research symposiums, school outreach, volunteering, undergraduate scholarships and much, much more.
summary: Women in Science and Engineering (WISE) is a University of Calgary student organization dedicated to supporting and encouraging women who are interested or engaged in science or engineering careers. WISE works towards fostering an academic environment ...

name: Young Pipeliners Association of Canada UCalgary
description: The Young Energy Infrastructure Professionals' (YEIP) UCalgary's purpose is to provide value to Young Professionals, make an impact on the energy industry, develop organizational success, and enhance leadership involvement in the energy industry. YEIP UCalgary will facilitate networking, provide opportunities to learn the breadth and depth of the business, increase knowledge transfer and attempt to influence the direction of the industry by providing a young professional perspective to issues of importance.
summary: The Young Energy Infrastructure Professionals' (YEIP) UCalgary's purpose is to provide value to Young Professionals, to make an impact on the energy industry , develop organizational success, and enhance leadership involvement in Canadian energy industry

name: Youth Lifting Youth
description: Promoting youth engagement in community welfare, empowering vulnerable communities by supporting Maskan: a transitional home for women and children fleeing domestic violence, and uplifting marginalized youth individuals. Together, we create an inclusive society where young individuals can thrive regardless of their circumstances.
summary: Youth Lifting Youth exists aims to promote youth engagement in community welfare and empower vulnerable communities. This includes uplifting marginalized youth and supporting Maskan: a transitional home for women and children escaping domestic violence
